Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who attended at least three structured caregiver learning sessions in the six months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who attended at least three structured facilitated learning sessions in the six months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for sustained participation in adult learning as distinct from one-off or incidental attendance.
Indicator sources: Caring for the Caregiver (https://www.notion.so/Caring-for-the-Caregiver-32060bfb014e8093b98cdb963a20aec7?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report a meaningful increase in confidence in supporting their child's learning, measured using a validated scale at baseline and six months after programme enrolment
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who show a meaningful increase in self-reported confidence in supporting their child's learning between baseline and six months after programme enrolment, used as a pre-post outcome indicator for adult learning and empowerment programme effects.
Indicator sources: Caring for the Caregiver (https://www.notion.so/Caring-for-the-Caregiver-32060bfb014e8093b98cdb963a20aec7?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report at least one self-initiated or school-initiated dialogue with a teacher or school staff member about their child's learning, in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report engaging in at least one meaningful dialogue with a teacher or school staff member about their child's learning in the school term prior to the survey, used as a proxy for active caregiver participation in school-home communication.
Indicator sources: Parental Engagement: Listening First, Partnering Always (https://www.notion.so/Parental-Engagement-Listening-First-Partnering-Always-32060bfb014e807eaef2d749baf41836?pvs=21), Working with Parents to Support Children's Learning (https://www.notion.so/Working-with-Parents-to-Support-Children-s-Learning-32060bfb014e80fcb869f21d30681c32?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of schools where at least one documented change to school practice or policy can be evidenced as a direct response to formally collected caregiver input, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of schools where at least one formally collected caregiver input has resulted in a documented and evidenced change to school practice or policy, used as a proxy for the responsiveness of school leadership to caregiver voice.
Indicator sources: Parental Engagement: Listening First, Partnering Always (https://www.notion.so/Parental-Engagement-Listening-First-Partnering-Always-32060bfb014e807eaef2d749baf41836?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report feeling confident making informed decisions across at least three defined education-related decision domains, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report feeling confident making informed decisions across at least three defined education-related decision domains, used as a domain-specific confidence indicator for caregiver agency in educational decision-making.
Indicator sources:
The Role of Educators and Caregivers in Supporting Participation of Children with Disabilities in Educational
Decision Making
 (https://www.notion.so/The-Role-of-Educators-and-Caregivers-in-Supporting-Participation-of-Children-with-Disabilities-in-Ed-32160bfb014e80d68e1bc6c6f197eaaf?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report actively reading and engaging with their child's school diary at least once per week during the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report actively reading and engaging with their child's school diary content at least once per week during the school term prior to the survey, used as a proxy for meaningful caregiver participation in school-home communication.
Indicator sources: Use of School Diaries to track homework accomplishment on both parent and teacher side is widespread in many learning institutions for early education.  (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-School-Diaries-to-track-homework-accomplishment-on-both-parent-and-teacher-side-is-widespread-32160bfb014e800b8087e8d69c0f6290?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learner school diaries containing caregiver signatures at least once per week across the school term prior to the survey, verified through physical diary review
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learner school diaries that contain caregiver signatures at least once per week across the school term prior to the survey, used as a proxy for minimum caregiver compliance with the school-home communication system.
Indicator sources: Use of School Diaries to track homework accomplishment on both parent and teacher side is widespread in many learning institutions for early education.  (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-School-Diaries-to-track-homework-accomplishment-on-both-parent-and-teacher-side-is-widespread-32160bfb014e800b8087e8d69c0f6290?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report taking at least one identifiable action or decision regarding their child's learning or behaviour informed by school diary content, in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report taking at least one identifiable action or decision regarding their child's learning or behaviour that was informed by school diary content in the school term prior to the survey, used as a proxy for action-oriented caregiver engagement with school-home communication.
Indicator sources: Use of School Diaries to track homework accomplishment on both parent and teacher side is widespread in many learning institutions for early education.  (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-School-Diaries-to-track-homework-accomplishment-on-both-parent-and-teacher-side-is-widespread-32160bfb014e800b8087e8d69c0f6290?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report discussing information from their child's school diary with at least one other person in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report discussing information from their child's school diary with a family member, the child, or a school staff member in the school term prior to the survey, used as a proxy for communicative engagement with school-home information.
Indicator sources: Use of School Diaries to track homework accomplishment on both parent and teacher side is widespread in many learning institutions for early education.  (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-School-Diaries-to-track-homework-accomplishment-on-both-parent-and-teacher-side-is-widespread-32160bfb014e800b8087e8d69c0f6290?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Rate of teacher diary entries receiving a written caregiver response within one week, across the school term prior to the survey, verified through physical diary review
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of teacher diary entries that receive a written caregiver response within one week, used as a system-level rate of substantive caregiver engagement with school-home communication content across the school term.
Indicator sources: Use of School Diaries to track homework accomplishment on both parent and teacher side is widespread in many learning institutions for early education.  (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-School-Diaries-to-track-homework-accomplishment-on-both-parent-and-teacher-side-is-widespread-32160bfb014e800b8087e8d69c0f6290?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Rate of teacher diary entries receiving a caregiver signature within one week, across the school term prior to the survey, verified through physical diary review
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of teacher diary entries that receive a caregiver signature within one week, used as a system-level rate of minimum caregiver compliance with the school-home communication protocol across the school term.
Indicator sources: Use of School Diaries to track homework accomplishment on both parent and teacher side is widespread in many learning institutions for early education.  (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-School-Diaries-to-track-homework-accomplishment-on-both-parent-and-teacher-side-is-widespread-32160bfb014e800b8087e8d69c0f6290?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who report that household responsibilities did not cause them to miss school on more than two days in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who report that household responsibilities did not cause them to miss school on more than two days in the school term prior to the survey, used as a child-reported proxy for household demand interference with school attendance.
Indicator sources: We deserve better: Unlocking the power of social protection for women and girls. (https://www.notion.so/We-deserve-better-Unlocking-the-power-of-social-protection-for-women-and-girls-32260bfb014e80f9877cee373cc6a2b4?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who report having a defined study routine that they followed on at least four of five school days each week in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who report having a defined study routine that they followed on at least four of five school days each week in the school term prior to the survey, used as a proxy for routine stability as a structural home condition supporting consistent home-based learning.
Indicator sources: Study Plans Promote Daily Review (https://www.notion.so/Study-Plans-Promote-Daily-Review-32260bfb014e80deb416f31911cdcc8e?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of enrolled learners from programme-defined vulnerable groups who achieved an attendance rate of at least 80 percent in the most recent completed school term, verified through attendance records
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of enrolled learners from programme-defined vulnerable groups who achieved an attendance rate of at least 80 percent in the most recent completed school term, used as a sustained participation indicator for whether inclusion of vulnerable learners is maintained beyond enrolment.
Indicator sources: SDG 4: Quality Education (https://www.notion.so/SDG-4-Quality-Education-32260bfb014e80908779e189463037dc?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of enrolled learners from programme-defined vulnerable groups who completed the school year and progressed to the next grade, in the most recent completed school year
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of enrolled learners from programme-defined vulnerable groups who completed the school year and progressed to the next grade, used as a structural inclusion outcome indicator of whether vulnerable learners are being retained in the education system.
Indicator sources: SDG 4: Quality Education (https://www.notion.so/SDG-4-Quality-Education-32260bfb014e80908779e189463037dc?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who report feeling free from fear, humiliation, and intimidation in the programme learning space in the school term prior to the survey, measured on a structured instrument
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who report feeling free from fear, humiliation, and intimidation in the programme learning space in the school term prior to the survey, used as a perceptual safety indicator for the emotional dimension of learning environment quality.
Sub-themes: Safety, dignity and respect in learning spaces (https://www.notion.so/Safety-dignity-and-respect-in-learning-spaces-31260bfb014e80ac91f0cad9217af2fc?pvs=21)
Indicator sources:
Five essential pillars for promoting
and protecting mental health and
psychosocial well-being in schools and learning environments (https://www.notion.so/Five-essential-pillars-for-promoting-and-protecting-mental-health-and-psychosocial-well-being-in-sch-32260bfb014e80109c2eea91937ba87e?pvs=21), Safe to Learn: creating safe and
enabling learning environments by
scaling Social and Behaviour Change
 (https://www.notion.so/Safe-to-Learn-creating-safe-and-enabling-learning-environments-by-scaling-Social-and-Behaviour-Chan-32260bfb014e80c3be9df203bf58bf6e?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who report feeling free from physical harm, threat, or violence within the programme learning space in the school term prior to the survey, measured on a structured instrument
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who report feeling free from physical harm, threat, or violence within the programme learning space in the school term prior to the survey, used as a perceptual physical safety indicator for the in-space dimension of learning environment quality.
Sub-themes: Safety, dignity and respect in learning spaces (https://www.notion.so/Safety-dignity-and-respect-in-learning-spaces-31260bfb014e80ac91f0cad9217af2fc?pvs=21)
Indicator sources:
Five essential pillars for promoting
and protecting mental health and
psychosocial well-being in schools and learning environments (https://www.notion.so/Five-essential-pillars-for-promoting-and-protecting-mental-health-and-psychosocial-well-being-in-sch-32260bfb014e80109c2eea91937ba87e?pvs=21), Safe to Learn: creating safe and
enabling learning environments by
scaling Social and Behaviour Change
 (https://www.notion.so/Safe-to-Learn-creating-safe-and-enabling-learning-environments-by-scaling-Social-and-Behaviour-Chan-32260bfb014e80c3be9df203bf58bf6e?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners scoring above the agreed threshold on a self-efficacy/self-worth scale at baseline and endline.
Indicator descriptor: Measures change in self-belief using a defined scale threshold.
Indicator sources: SDG 4: Quality Education (https://www.notion.so/SDG-4-Quality-Education-32260bfb014e80908779e189463037dc?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who agree with the statement “I can succeed in my learning when I try.”
Indicator descriptor: Measures academic self-efficacy/growth-oriented belief.
Indicator sources: SDG 4: Quality Education (https://www.notion.so/SDG-4-Quality-Education-32260bfb014e80908779e189463037dc?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners reporting they have at least one trusted adult in the learning environment.
Indicator descriptor: Measures relational protection and help-seeking anchor.
Indicator sources:
Safe to Learn: creating safe and
enabling learning environments by
scaling Social and Behaviour Change
 (https://www.notion.so/Safe-to-Learn-creating-safe-and-enabling-learning-environments-by-scaling-Social-and-Behaviour-Chan-32260bfb014e80c3be9df203bf58bf6e?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners reporting they felt emotionally safe in school or programme spaces on most days in the last month.
Indicator descriptor: Measures consistency, not just occasional emotional safety.
Indicator sources:
Safe to Learn: creating safe and
enabling learning environments by
scaling Social and Behaviour Change
 (https://www.notion.so/Safe-to-Learn-creating-safe-and-enabling-learning-environments-by-scaling-Social-and-Behaviour-Chan-32260bfb014e80c3be9df203bf58bf6e?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who can name a specific next educational level, qualification, or institution they are working toward, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who can articulate a concrete, bounded educational goal at the time of survey, used as a proxy for educational future orientation and planning capacity.
Indicator sources: SDG 4: Quality Education (https://www.notion.so/SDG-4-Quality-Education-32260bfb014e80908779e189463037dc?pvs=21), Global Framework on Transferable Skills (https://www.notion.so/Global-Framework-on-Transferable-Skills-32260bfb014e80609863ebd11f9ec955?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who can name a specific occupation, type of enterprise, or income-generating activity they are actively preparing for, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who can articulate a concrete, bounded livelihood goal they are actively preparing for at the time of survey, used as a proxy for livelihood future orientation and vocational planning capacity.
Indicator sources: SDG 4: Quality Education (https://www.notion.so/SDG-4-Quality-Education-32260bfb014e80908779e189463037dc?pvs=21), Global Framework on Transferable Skills (https://www.notion.so/Global-Framework-on-Transferable-Skills-32260bfb014e80609863ebd11f9ec955?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who report using at least two strategies from a programme-defined adaptive coping strategy list when facing school-related stress in the school term prior to the survey, verified through a naming and description prompt
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who report using at least two strategies from a programme-defined adaptive coping strategy list when facing school-related stress in the school term prior to the survey, used as a coping repertoire breadth indicator for whether learners have and use multiple adaptive coping strategies rather than relying on a single approach or maladaptive alternatives.

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who can articulate at least three distinct personal strengths, evidenced through a facilitated structured reflection tool, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who can name and describe at least three distinct personal strengths during a facilitated reflection exercise at the time of survey, used as a proxy for self-awareness and strengths-based self-concept.
Indicator sources: Global Framework on Transferable Skills (https://www.notion.so/Global-Framework-on-Transferable-Skills-32260bfb014e80609863ebd11f9ec955?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who report a positive sense of self-worth across at least two programme-defined identity dimensions, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who report feeling positively about who they are across a defined set of identity dimensions relevant to the programme context, used as a proxy for affective self-worth and identity affirmation.
Indicator sources:
Five essential pillars for promoting
and protecting mental health and
psychosocial well-being in schools and learning environments (https://www.notion.so/Five-essential-pillars-for-promoting-and-protecting-mental-health-and-psychosocial-well-being-in-sch-32260bfb014e80109c2eea91937ba87e?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners with no disruption in caregiving arrangement, shelter, or schooling in the last 6 months.
Indicator descriptor: Measures educational and caregiving stability.

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who have experienced disruption in caregiving arrangement, shelter, or schooling in the last 6 months.
Indicator descriptor: Measures exposure to instability affecting learning continuity.

Indicator statement: Number of school days missed due to housing, safety, or caregiving instability per learner per term.
Indicator descriptor: Measures intensity of instability-related learning loss.

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who report holding or fulfilling a named peer leadership or mentoring role in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who report holding or fulfilling a named peer leadership or mentoring role in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for active leadership contribution and relational influence within the peer group or community.
Indicator sources: Skills for the Future Global Platform        A global platform for voluntary commitments in youth skills development (https://www.notion.so/Skills-for-the-Future-Global-Platform-A-global-platform-for-voluntary-commitments-in-youth-sk-32260bfb014e80bb88aec73b6cb8c3cc?pvs=21), Global Framework on Transferable Skills (https://www.notion.so/Global-Framework-on-Transferable-Skills-32260bfb014e80609863ebd11f9ec955?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who report perceiving that their ideas shaped the direction or outcome of a group, school, or community activity in the six months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who report perceiving that their ideas shaped the direction or outcome of a group, school, or community activity in the six months prior to the survey, used as a perceptual influence indicator complementing the evidenced influence measure in indicator 2b.

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who report having a structured opportunity to contribute ideas or suggestions in a defined group, school, or community setting in the six months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who report having a structured opportunity to contribute ideas or suggestions in a defined group, school, or community setting in the six months prior to the survey, used as a voice access indicator distinct from whether those ideas were acted upon.

Indicator statement: Proportion of total programme participants who belong to each pre-defined priority group as identified in the programme equity targeting framework, in the reporting year
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of total programme participants who belong to each pre-defined priority group as identified in the programme equity targeting framework, used as an annual participation coverage indicator for equity-targeted programme reach.

Indicator statement: Proportion of identified participation barriers that have a documented mitigation plan within 12 months of identification, verified through barrier monitoring records
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of participation barriers identified through the programme's barrier monitoring process that have a documented mitigation plan within 12 months of identification, used as a planning responsiveness indicator for the barrier reduction pipeline.

Indicator statement: Proportion of logged community feedback cases that received a first response within the programme-defined service level agreement timeframe, in the reporting period
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of logged community feedback cases that received a first response within the programme-defined service level agreement timeframe, used as a system-level indicator of the responsiveness of the feedback and accountability mechanism.

Indicator statement: Proportion of targeted decision-makers on the programme stakeholder map who received at least one programme-produced evidence product in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of decision-makers on the programme stakeholder map who received at least one programme-produced evidence product in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a dissemination coverage rate for evidence-based influencing efforts.

Indicator statement: Proportion of targeted policy, planning, or institutional changes in the programme influencing plan that were achieved and evidenced as directly informed by programme evidence or stakeholder engagement in the reporting year
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of policy, planning, or institutional changes identified in the programme influencing plan that were achieved and can be evidenced as directly informed by programme evidence or stakeholder engagement in the reporting year, used as an outcome indicator of the programme's systems-level influence.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme partners who have formally adopted and are actively reporting against the common programme indicator set in the current reporting period
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of programme partners who have formally adopted the common programme indicator set and are actively reporting against it in the current reporting period, used as a proxy for alignment of measurement frameworks across the partner portfolio.

Indicator statement: Proportion of planned joint learning or evidence review meetings that were convened in the programme year, verified through meeting records
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of planned joint learning or evidence review meetings that were actually convened in the programme year, used as a regularity indicator of collective reflection practice across the programme and its partners.

Indicator statement: Perceived influence of community input on decisions
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members perceive that their views, concerns, or contributions influence decisions affecting the community. The indicator captures whether participation processes are seen as meaningful and whether community input is believed to shape final decisions rather than being merely consultative.
Sub-themes: Perceived Inclusiveness and Representation in Village Decision-Making (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Inclusiveness-and-Representation-in-Village-Decision-Making-31b60bfb014e809bbfbfd2029225ca6c?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived inclusion of diverse groups in decision-making
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members perceive that different social groups within the community, such as women, youth, or other marginalized groups, are included in decision-making processes. The indicator captures perceptions of representational inclusiveness and whether decision-making reflects the perspectives of a broad range of community members.
Sub-themes: Perceived Inclusiveness and Representation in Village Decision-Making (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Inclusiveness-and-Representation-in-Village-Decision-Making-31b60bfb014e809bbfbfd2029225ca6c?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived inclusiveness and voice in community decision-making
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members perceive that they have meaningful opportunities to participate in decision-making processes related to the intervention or community activities. The indicator captures whether individuals feel able to attend discussions, share their views, and contribute to decisions that affect their community.
Sub-themes: Perceived Inclusiveness and Representation in Village Decision-Making (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Inclusiveness-and-Representation-in-Village-Decision-Making-31b60bfb014e809bbfbfd2029225ca6c?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived favouritism in benefit distribution
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members perceive that the benefits generated by an intervention favour certain households, groups, or individuals over others. The indicator captures perceptions of partiality or favouritism in benefit allocation, which can undermine the perceived fairness and legitimacy of an intervention.
Sub-themes: Perceived Fairness of Benefit Distribution Within the Village (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Fairness-of-Benefit-Distribution-Within-the-Village-31b60bfb014e808d909afeb5ab9bd0be?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived inclusion of vulnerable households in benefit allocation
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members perceive that poorer or more vulnerable households are included in the distribution of benefits generated by an intervention. The indicator captures perceptions of equitable benefit allocation, specifically whether those with greater need are able to access or receive project benefits rather than being excluded from them.
Sub-themes: Perceived Fairness of Benefit Distribution Within the Village (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Fairness-of-Benefit-Distribution-Within-the-Village-31b60bfb014e808d909afeb5ab9bd0be?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived fairness of benefit distribution
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members perceive that the benefits generated by an intervention are distributed fairly across households within the community. The indicator captures perceptions of distributive justice and whether benefits are seen as equitably shared rather than concentrated among particular individuals, families, or groups.
Sub-themes: Perceived Fairness of Benefit Distribution Within the Village (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Fairness-of-Benefit-Distribution-Within-the-Village-31b60bfb014e808d909afeb5ab9bd0be?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived increase in community tensions associated with the intervention
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members perceive that the intervention has increased tensions, disagreements, or conflict within the community. The indicator captures whether the intervention is perceived to strain social relationships or create divisions between individuals or groups.
Sub-themes: Perceived Social Burdens and Tensions Associated with the Intervention (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Social-Burdens-and-Tensions-Associated-with-the-Intervention-31b60bfb014e80109a82d2a5f0217410?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived exclusion or negative impacts on some community members
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members perceive that the intervention excludes, disadvantages, or negatively affects certain individuals or groups within the community. The indicator captures perceptions that some people may experience harms, losses, or unfair treatment associated with the intervention.
Sub-themes: Perceived Social Burdens and Tensions Associated with the Intervention (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Social-Burdens-and-Tensions-Associated-with-the-Intervention-31b60bfb014e80109a82d2a5f0217410?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived burdens or restrictions associated with intervention rules
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members perceive that the rules, requirements, or enforcement mechanisms associated with the intervention create burdens, restrictions, or pressures for community members. The indicator captures whether participation in or compliance with the intervention is experienced as difficult, restrictive, or stressful.
Sub-themes: Perceived Social Burdens and Tensions Associated with the Intervention (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Social-Burdens-and-Tensions-Associated-with-the-Intervention-31b60bfb014e80109a82d2a5f0217410?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived Integrity of Intervention Implementers
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members believe that those responsible for implementing the intervention act honestly, follow agreed rules, and manage resources in a transparent and accountable manner.
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived Goodwill of Implementers Toward the Community
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members believe that intervention implementers act in good faith and seek to advance the interests and wellbeing of the community.
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived Responsiveness of Implementers to Community Concerns
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members believe that intervention implementers listen to, consider, and respond appropriately to concerns raised by community members.
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived Continuity of Intervention Benefits
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members believe that the benefits generated by the intervention will continue over time without interruption, reduction, or sudden withdrawal.
Sub-themes: Perceived Continuity of the Intervention and Its Benefits (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Continuity-of-the-Intervention-and-Its-Benefits-31b60bfb014e804fae8cc060772420b6?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Confidence that the Intervention Will Remain in Place
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members believe that the intervention itself will continue to operate in the community over the coming years.
Sub-themes: Perceived Continuity of the Intervention and Its Benefits (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Continuity-of-the-Intervention-and-Its-Benefits-31b60bfb014e804fae8cc060772420b6?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Perceived Risk of Sudden Interruption of Intervention Benefits
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members are concerned that the intervention or its benefits could stop unexpectedly or be withdrawn without warning.
Sub-themes: Perceived Continuity of the Intervention and Its Benefits (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Continuity-of-the-Intervention-and-Its-Benefits-31b60bfb014e804fae8cc060772420b6?pvs=21)
Indicator sources: Social assessment of conservation initiatives: A review of rapid methodologies (https://www.notion.so/Social-assessment-of-conservation-initiatives-A-review-of-rapid-methodologies-31e60bfb014e8092b085c64943831d2a?pvs=21)

Indicator descriptor: The expected length of time over which the outcome is anticipated to continue generating benefits for the respondent or their household.

Indicator descriptor: The financial value that the respondent associates with the outcome being assessed.

Indicator descriptor: The respondent’s estimate of how widely the outcome has been experienced among other households or individuals in the community.

Indicator statement: Number of administrative offices completed within the project area
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of administrative office facilities completed within the project area over a defined reporting period. Within the theme of access to social services and the subtheme of social infrastructure development, administrative offices are understood as facilities that support the planning, coordination, and delivery of social services to project-affected communities.

Indicator statement: Number of carbon-finance-supported school support programs launched within the project area
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of school support programs — including but not limited to lunch programs and learning kit distributions — launched with carbon finance support within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Number of existing healthcare facilities rehabilitated within the project area
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of existing healthcare facilities — including clinics and health posts — that have undergone rehabilitation or repair works within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Number of new healthcare facilities constructed from scratch with carbon finance support within the project area
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of new healthcare facilities — including clinics and health posts — constructed from scratch with carbon finance revenues within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Number of medical staff offices created to support patient consultation.
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of dedicated medical staff consultation offices created with carbon finance revenues to support patient consultation within carbon-finance-supported healthcare facilities in the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Number of monthly patient visits per carbon-finance-supported healthcare facility within the project area
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the average number of patient visits recorded per month at healthcare facilities constructed or rehabilitated with carbon finance revenues within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Number of new classrooms fully constructed and roofed within the project area
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of new classrooms that have been fully constructed and roofed within carbon-finance-supported school infrastructure within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Number of carbon-finance-supported school and community latrines built and functional within the project area
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of latrines constructed with carbon finance revenues at schools and community facilities within the project area that are confirmed functional over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Number of carbon-finance-supported storage tanks constructed within the project area.
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of water storage tanks constructed with carbon finance revenues within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Number of carbon-finance-supported teacher houses completed within the project area
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of teacher residential houses completed with carbon finance revenues within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Number of carbon-finance-supported water points installed within the project area
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total number of water points, including boreholes and taps, installed with carbon finance revenues within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Percentage of households in the project area using carbon-finance-supported improved water points
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the proportion of households within the project area that report using carbon-finance-supported improved water points as a primary or secondary water source over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Proportion of eligible households accessing affordable housing constructed through carbon-funded investments
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures whether affordable housing constructed through carbon-funded investments is reaching its intended beneficiaries. It is an outcome indicator, linking housing construction investments to beneficiary access.

Indicator statement: Proportion of intended community members using community facilities constructed through carbon-funded investments
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures whether community facilities constructed through carbon-funded investments are being used by the populations they were intended to serve. It is an outcome/usage indicator, linking facility construction investments to actual uptake by intended beneficiaries.

Indicator statement: Proportion of school-aged children enrolled in the school feeding program.
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the proportion of school-aged children within the project area who are enrolled in a school feeding program supported by carbon finance revenues over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Proportion of students attending classes in carbon-finance-supported newly constructed or improved school facilities during the current school term
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the proportion of students within the project area who are attending classes in school facilities newly constructed or improved with carbon finance revenues during the current school term.

Indicator statement: Proportion of underserved population in project-targeted areas with improved access to public transportation following carbon-funded public transport infrastructure investments
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures whether carbon-funded public transport infrastructure investments are associated with improved access to public transportation in the communities they are intended to serve. It is an outcome indicator, linking infrastructure spending to changes in transport access

Indicator statement: Total carbon finance disbursed for construction or upgrading of public transport infrastructure
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total amount of carbon finance revenues actually disbursed for the construction or upgrading of public transport infrastructure within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Total carbon finance disbursed for construction or upgrading of social protection centers
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total amount of carbon finance revenues actually disbursed for the construction or upgrading of social protection centers within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Total carbon finance disbursed for construction/upgrading of emergency service facilities
Indicator descriptor: This indicator tracks the monetary value of carbon finance revenues allocated and disbursed specifically for the construction or upgrading of emergency service facilities (e.g. fire stations, ambulance posts, disaster response centers) within the project area during a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Total carbon finance disbursed for the construction of affordable housing
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total amount of carbon finance revenues actually disbursed for the construction of affordable housing units within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Total carbon finance disbursed for the construction of community facilities.
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total amount of carbon finance revenues actually disbursed for the construction of community facilities, including community centers, multipurpose halls, youth centers, and other spaces designated for organized community activities, within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Total carbon finance disbursed for the construction of new or improved healthcare facility infrastructure
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total amount of carbon finance revenues actually disbursed for the construction of new healthcare facilities built from scratch and the rehabilitation or improvement of existing healthcare facility infrastructure within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Total carbon finance disbursed for the construction of new school infrastructure or improvement of existing infrastructure.
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total amount of carbon finance revenues actually disbursed for the construction of new school infrastructure or the improvement of existing school infrastructure within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Total carbon finance disbursed for the construction of water access centers
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total amount of carbon finance revenues actually disbursed for the construction of water access centers within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Total carbon finance disbursed for the construction of water treatment plants
Indicator descriptor: This indicator measures the total amount of carbon finance revenues actually disbursed for the construction of water treatment plants within the project area over a defined reporting period.

Indicator statement: Proportion of low-income households in the project area utilizing verified financial support (subsidies, fee waivers, or social protection schemes) to access healthcare services.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks whether individuals and households can access subsidies, fee waivers, or social protection schemes to reduce the cost of healthcare services

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals with access to financial tools (e.g., savings groups, microloans, health insurance) to afford essential services that provide social protection floors/systems.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks whether individuals and households can access subsidies, fee waivers, or social protection schemes to reduce the cost of education services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals with access to financial tools (e.g., savings groups, microloans, health insurance) to afford essential services that provide social protection floors/systems.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks whether individuals and households can access subsidies, fee waivers, or social protection schemes to reduce the cost of education services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals with access to financial tools (e.g., savings groups, microloans, health insurance) to afford essential services that provide social protection floors/systems.
Indicator descriptor: Measure of household economic resilience and the strength of local safety nets. This assesses whether families have the liquid resources or credit facilities necessary to withstand life shocks (like illness or crop failure) without falling into a cycle of debt or poverty. It evaluates the shift from financial vulnerability to household-level stability, ensuring that "social protection floors" are not just theoretical policies, but accessible realities that allow the most marginalized to afford the dignity of essential services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals with access to financial tools (e.g., savings groups, microloans, health insurance) to afford essential services that provide social protection floors/systems.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks whether individuals and households can access subsidies, fee waivers, or social protection schemes to reduce the cost of healthcare.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households reporting improved access to education services due to financial support programs
Indicator descriptor: Tracks whether individuals and households can access subsidies, fee waivers, or social protection schemes to reduce the cost of education.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households reporting improved access to healthcare services due to financial support programs
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which individuals or households use financial tools (e.g., savings groups, microloans, health insurance) to afford essential services.
Measures the extent to which individuals or households use financial tools (e.g., savings groups, microloans, health insurance) to afford essential services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of school-aged children from low-income households in the project area receiving verified scholarships, stipends, or fee waivers that cover direct education costs (tuition, materials, transportation).
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which individuals or households use financial tools (e.g., savings groups, microloans, health insurance) to afford essential services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of school-aged children from low-income households in the project area receiving verified scholarships, stipends, or fee waivers that cover direct education costs (tuition, materials, transportation).
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which individuals or households use financial tools (e.g., savings groups, microloans, health insurance) to afford essential services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households in remote or underserved areas who report having physical access to healthcare within a reasonable distance.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of households in remote or underserved areas who report having physical access to healthcare within a reasonable distance.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals that indicate the transportation available for reaching different essential social services such as hopsitals and schools is on time and can be frequently accessed when needed
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether transportation infrastructure (e.g., roads, public transport, bicycles) is available and functional to enable access to essential services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals that indicate the transportation available for reaching different essential social services such as hopsitals and schools is safe
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether transportation infrastructure (e.g., roads, public transport, bicycles) is available and functional to enable access to essential services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals that devot or spend more than 10% of its expenditure to meeting its mobility and transportation needs in accessing essential social services
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether transportation infrastructure (e.g., roads, public transport, bicycles) is available and functional to enable access to essential services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals with physical access to one or more forms of public transport networks they can use to access or reach different social services such as healthcare services, schools and social protection services.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether transportation infrastructure (e.g., roads, public transport, bicycles) is available and functional to enable access to essential services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals with physical access to one or more forms of transportation they can use to access or reach different social services such as healthcare services, schools and social protection services.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether transportation infrastructure (e.g., roads, public transport, bicycles) is available and functional to enable access to essential services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals in remote areas  proportion of households in remote or underserved areas who report having physical access to social protection services  within a reasonable distance.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of households in remote or underserved areas who report having physical access to healthcare, education, and social protection services within a reasonable distance.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals in remote areas  proportion of households in remote or underserved areas who report having physical access to education services within a reasonable distance.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of households in remote or underserved areas who report having physical access to healthcare, education, and social protection services within a reasonable distance.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals in remote areas  proportion of households in remote or underserved areas who report having physical access to healthcare services within a reasonable distance.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of households in remote or underserved areas who report having physical access to healthcare, education, and social protection services within a reasonable distance.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals who can name a healthcare service facility near them where they can access necessary healthcare services
Indicator descriptor: Measures community awareness of proximate healthcare facilities, including hospitals, clinics, health centers, or other medical service points. This indicator reflects whether populations know where to seek medical care when needed and serves as a precondition for healthcare utilization. Low scores may indicate gaps in health system communication, inadequate signage, or insufficient community outreach about available health services.

Indicator statement: Proportion of reached individuals or households  who report that the information made it easier for them or others in the community to use services.
Proportion of the individuals or households  who report receiving information about public services through community outreach efforts in the last year
The proportion of surveyed individuals (or household representatives) who are aware of a specific service and can correctly describe at least two of the three key steps required to access it.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of community members who are aware that essential services (e.g., healthcare, education, social protection) exist and understand how to access and use them.

Indicator statement: Proportion of surveyed individuals (or household representatives) who are aware of a specific service and can correctly describe at least two of the three key steps required to access it.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of community members who are aware that essential services (e.g., healthcare, education, social protection) exist and understand how to access and use them.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals who can name a public or private facility where they can access social protection services
Indicator descriptor: Measures knowledge of facilities providing social protection services such as social welfare offices, cash transfer programs, disability services, pension offices, or community support centers. This indicator reveals whether vulnerable populations know where to access safety net programs and social assistance. Low awareness can significantly impede uptake of social protection schemes, even when individuals are eligible, highlighting needs for targeted information campaigns and community-based outreach.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals who can name a school or education institution where they or their children can access education services of their specific level
Indicator descriptor: Assesses household knowledge of appropriate educational facilities matching their or their children's education level (primary, secondary, tertiary, vocational, etc.). This indicator captures whether families know where to enroll or continue education and reflects the effectiveness of education sector information dissemination. It identifies potential enrollment barriers related to awareness rather than access, particularly important for vulnerable populations or in areas with dispersed services.

Indicator statement: Percentage of Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities (IPLCs) who report having received comprehensive information on project risks, benefits, and legal implications at least 90 days prior to the scheduled consent vote or agreement signing.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the sufficiency and timeliness of information disclosure prior to formal decision-making.

Indicator descriptor: Measures the depth of community comprehension regarding long-term obligations and the technical financial structures that govern carbon revenue distribution.

Indicator statement: Number of project design modifications or boundary changes formally documented as a direct result of IPLC feedback during the pre-implementation phase, demonstrating that their right to reject or modify activities was upheld.

Sub-themes: Clarity about the short-term and long-term benefits of the project (https://www.notion.so/Clarity-about-the-short-term-and-long-term-benefits-of-the-project-33060bfb014e801291ccf8ce51df78bf?pvs=21)

Indicator descriptor: The extent to which community members believe that benefits are distributed equitably across different groups, without discrimination or exclusion based on gender, ethnicity, social status, or other factors.

Indicator descriptor: This indicator assesses whether the project has a formally documented benefit sharing plan that transparently defines how revenues, carbon credits, or other benefits generated from activities will be distributed among stakeholders.
Sub-themes: Clarity about the short-term and long-term benefits of the project (https://www.notion.so/Clarity-about-the-short-term-and-long-term-benefits-of-the-project-33060bfb014e801291ccf8ce51df78bf?pvs=21), Participatory Benefit-Sharing Design (https://www.notion.so/Participatory-Benefit-Sharing-Design-33060bfb014e803b8fb0e0593c6612b4?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members (disaggregated by gender, ethnicity, and socio-economic status) who can correctly answer key questions about the benefit sharing mechanism design, eligibility criteria, decision-making process, and range of benefits during a structured survey.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which community members understand how the project’s benefit-sharing mechanism works, including how benefits are allocated, who is eligible, and how decisions are made. It also assesses awareness of the range of benefits—both short-term and long-term—that the project is expected to deliver, ensuring transparency and informed participation
Sub-themes: Clarity about the short-term and long-term benefits of the project (https://www.notion.so/Clarity-about-the-short-term-and-long-term-benefits-of-the-project-33060bfb014e801291ccf8ce51df78bf?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members (or household representatives) who agree that marginalized groups are receiving a fair share of the project's benefits
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which benefit-sharing mechanisms actively include and equitably distribute benefits to marginalized groups, such as women, Indigenous Peoples, persons with disabilities, and other vulnerable populations. This includes assessing whether targeted interventions are in place, whether marginalized groups are represented in decision-making, and whether mechanisms exist to monitor and prevent exclusion or elite capture.
Sub-themes: Plans in place to ensure that marginalized groups benefit (https://www.notion.so/Plans-in-place-to-ensure-that-marginalized-groups-benefit-33060bfb014e8050b553eb80646eb378?pvs=21)

Potential
  negative unintended consequences considered & mitigated (https://www.notion.so/Potential-negative-unintended-consequences-considered-mitigated-31260bfb014e80d5b0d3dcfb544f159a?pvs=21)

Potential
  negative unintended consequences considered & mitigated (https://www.notion.so/Potential-negative-unintended-consequences-considered-mitigated-31260bfb014e80d5b0d3dcfb544f159a?pvs=21)

Potential
  negative unintended consequences considered & mitigated (https://www.notion.so/Potential-negative-unintended-consequences-considered-mitigated-31260bfb014e80d5b0d3dcfb544f159a?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Percentage of identified benefit sharing related social risks with mitigation actions implemented
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether social risks linked to benefit sharing, such as exclusion, elite capture, conflict, or inequality, are not only identified but also addressed through documented mitigation actions.
Sub-themes: Potential negative unintended consequences considered & mitigated (https://www.notion.so/Potential-negative-unintended-consequences-considered-mitigated-33060bfb014e80ee9e5ec86b2bd2cf98?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Percentage of benefit sharing related grievances resolved within the reporting period
Indicator descriptor: Measures how effectively the project responds to complaints or adverse incidents arising from benefit sharing arrangements.
Sub-themes: Potential negative unintended consequences considered & mitigated (https://www.notion.so/Potential-negative-unintended-consequences-considered-mitigated-33060bfb014e80ee9e5ec86b2bd2cf98?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Existence of a documented risk register for the benefit sharing mechanism
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether the project has a formal, up to date risk register that identifies, categorizes, and tracks risks related to the benefit sharing mechanism, including exclusion, conflict, elite capture, inequity, or other unintended harms.
Sub-themes: Potential negative unintended consequences considered & mitigated (https://www.notion.so/Potential-negative-unintended-consequences-considered-mitigated-33060bfb014e80ee9e5ec86b2bd2cf98?pvs=21), Participatory Benefit-Sharing Design (https://www.notion.so/Participatory-Benefit-Sharing-Design-33060bfb014e803b8fb0e0593c6612b4?pvs=21)

Access
  to Financial Services & Asset Building (https://www.notion.so/Access-to-Financial-Services-Asset-Building-31260bfb014e8065a191fdc359c3ee1d?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of individuals and households (disaggregated by gender, age, income level, and livelihood type) with access to and active use of formal and informal financial services, and demonstrating progress in asset accumulation.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks the number of people accessing financial services, including microfinance and credit.
Sub-themes: Access to Financial Services & Asset Building (https://www.notion.so/Access-to-Financial-Services-Asset-Building-33060bfb014e80f59bd2c8214623525d?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of households who have the financial resilience to cope with unexpected economic shocks (e.g., medical emergencies, job loss, crop failure) without falling into debt or hardship.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether households have the financial resilience to cope with unexpected economic shocks (e.g., medical emergencies, job loss, crop failure) without falling into debt or hardship.
Sub-themes: Access to Financial Services & Asset Building (https://www.notion.so/Access-to-Financial-Services-Asset-Building-33060bfb014e80f59bd2c8214623525d?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of project beneficiaries (disaggregated by gender, age, income level, and livelihood type) who have participated in financial literacy training, savings programs, or financial management initiatives provided or facilitated by the project/programme.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks community engagement in financial literacy training, savings groups, and financial management initiatives
Sub-themes: Access to Financial Services & Asset Building (https://www.notion.so/Access-to-Financial-Services-Asset-Building-33060bfb014e80f59bd2c8214623525d?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of project beneficiaries (disaggregated by gender, age, and vulnerability status) who are in stable employment that provides fair wages, benefits, and job security in compliance with decent work standards.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the numbers of individuals in stable, formal employment that provides fair wages, benefits, and job security in compliance with labor rights and decent work standards.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members who perceive that marginalized groups (women, Indigenous Peoples, persons with disabilities, youth, elderly) have equal access to employment opportunities created or facilitated by the project.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the numbers of individuals in stable, formal employment that provides fair wages, benefits, and job security in compliance with labor rights and decent work standards.

Indicator statement: Proportion of marginalized groups (e.g., women, Indigenous Peoples, persons with disabilities) have access to employment opportunities as a result of the project.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks the extent to which marginalized groups (e.g., women, Indigenous Peoples, persons with disabilities) have access to employment opportunities as a result of the project.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households or individuals in project areas that have experienced an increase in income and improved financial stability.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the number of households that have experienced an increase in income and improved financial stability.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households in the project areas that have reported improvements in living standards, savings, and reduced economic vulnerability due to improved income levels
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether increased household income has led to improvements in living standards, savings, and reduced economic vulnerability at the household level.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households in the communities within project areas experiencing improved living as a result of reduced household poverty
Indicator descriptor: Tracks the broader effects on the community as a result of reduced household poverty.

Indicator statement: Percentage of target households that have successfully diversified their income by engaging in at least two distinct, non-extractive income-generating activities (IGAs) supported or facilitated by the project.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the number of households engaged in multiple income-generating activities, capturing the prevalence of diversification as a strategy for economic stability

Indicator statement: Proportion of households reporting a reduction in economic vulnerability, evidenced by the ability to cover an unexpected emergency expense (equal to 1 month’s income) through savings or diversified income, within [X] years of project start.
Indicator descriptor: Evaluates whether engaging in multiple income-generating activities has improved household financial stability, reduced economic vulnerability, and strengthened resilience to external shocks.

Indicator statement: Number and percentage of households within the project area that have established at least one new nature-positive micro-enterprise or self-employment activity facilitated by project interventions.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the number of households with at least one self-employed individual or business owner.

Indicator statement: Extent to which the local economy has diversified, measured by a reduction in the community-level 'Dependency Ratio' on primary extractive resources (e.g., logging, charcoal) due to the growth of new income sources.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether self-employment and business growth have strengthened economic resilience by creating new income sources, reducing dependency, and improving financial security at the community level.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members (disaggregated by gender and marginalized group status) actively participating in project-led cooperatives, micro-finance schemes, or entrepreneurship training programs.
Indicator descriptor: Monitors community engagement in training, cooperatives, financing, or other initiatives designed to support self-employment and entrepreneurship.

Indicator statement: Percentage increase in total household income attributable to project-supported self-employment and business activities, compared to the project baseline.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which self-employment and business activities have contributed to increased household income and economic stability.

Indicator statement: Socio-economic baseline survey completed and peer reviewed.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether a socio-economic baseline survey has been conducted, finalized, and validated through peer review to establish baseline conditions for communities in the project area.
Theme: Preparatory Planning, Economic Empowerment & Resilient Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Number and percentage of community members (disaggregated by gender and youth) formally employed or volunteering in project conservation roles (e.g., forest patrols, nursery management, or bio-monitoring) per annum.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which communities actively engaged in conservation programs and initiatives.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage of households (disaggregated by gender and marginalized status) who report that the rules for 'Sustainable Yield Limits' were co-designed through their traditional knowledge and that they have fair, non-discriminatory access to the harvesting zones.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks the extent to which local communities engage in sustainable harvesting and resource management practices
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Proportion of households reporting a reduction in annual out-of-pocket healthcare expenditures due to the maintained availability and sustainable use of traditional medicinal plants within protected forest zones.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses the extent to which households benefit from ecosystem services such as pollination, soil fertility, and access to medicinal plants.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage of project-area households reporting stable or increased agricultural productivity (yield per hectare) for target crops, attributed to improved pollination services and soil fertility resulting from conservation practices.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses the extent to which households benefit from ecosystem services such as pollination, soil fertility, and access to medicinal plants.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage of households (disaggregated by gender and accessibility needs) who receive, understand, and can demonstrate correct 'Early Action' protocols within 24 hours of a climate-hazard warning.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of households that are reached promptly after a climate-hard warning, correctly comprehend the prescribed early action steps, and are able to demonstrate the actions they should take within 24 hours. Disaggregation by gender and accessibility needs helps assess whether early warning communication and preparedness measures are inclusive and effective for different population groups.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage reduction in the value of household assets (crops, livestock, housing) lost per extreme weather event, compared to a baseline or non-project control community with similar hazard exposure.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which project-supported households experience lower economic losses in key household assets during extreme weather events relative to a baseline or a comparable non-project community exposed to similar hazards. This indicates the effectiveness of resilience, preparedness, and risk reduction measures in protecting household livelihoods and property.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage of households, businesses, and community institutions (e.g., schools, clinics) that can demonstrate active segregation of waste at the source and participation in formal recycling or circular economy programs.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of households, businesses, and community members actively engaging in sustainable waste disposal, recycling, and pollution prevention efforts.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage reduction in the volume of unmanaged waste (litter/open dumping) in key ecosystem zones (forests/rivers) following the implementation of localized waste collection and disposal infrastructure.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether waste management infrastructure (e.g., waste collection, disposal, recycling services) is effective in reducing pollution.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage reduction in household expenditure on fossil fuels (e.g., kerosene, diesel) and time spent on fuelwood collection, correlated with an increase in income-generating activities made possible by reliable energy access.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which improved access to reliable energy reduces household spending on fossil-based fuels and the time required to collect fuelwood, while also enabling greater participation in income-generating activities. This indicates both the economic and livelihood benefits of decentralized renewable energy access.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage of households and community institutions (e.g., clinics, schools) utilizing decentralized renewable energy systems (e.g., Solar Home Systems, mini-grids, or biogas) as their primary or secondary power source
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of households and community institutions that use decentralized renewable energy systems as a main or supplementary source of electricity or energy for daily needs. This reflects the extent of access to cleaner, more reliable, and locally available energy solutions within the target area.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage of project farmland showing measurable improvement in Soil Organic Carbon (SOC) and nutrient levels, correlated with a [X]% increase in crop yield per hectare.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of farmland where soil fertility has improved and assesses its impact on agricultural productivity.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Existence of a community-ratified Land Use Plan (LUP) supported by a dedicated budget and aligned with local/national government agricultural policies.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses the policies, programs, and funding available for community-led land, soil, and resource management initiatives.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage of farmers reporting timely and equitable access to the technical training, material inputs (e.g., bio-fertilizers), and financial credit required to sustain soil health programs.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks access to financial, technical, and material resources that support sustainable land use, including soil health programs and sustainable resource management initiatives.
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Proportion of local land users (disaggregated by gender) who have successfully transitioned to at least three sustainable farming methods (e.g., no-till, cover cropping, or integrated pest management).
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of farmers and land users adopting soil conservation techniques and sustainable farming methods
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Stability or increase in the seasonal flow and recharge rates of community-used water sources (springs, streams, wells), directly correlated with the protection and restoration of upstream forest and wetland buffers.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether the seasonal flow and groundwater recharge of community water sources are maintained or improved over time as a result of protecting and restoring upstream forests and wetland buffer areas. This reflects the role of ecosystem conservation in sustaining water availability for community use.
Sub-themes:
Water Security & Watershed Protection (https://www.notion.so/Water-Security-Watershed-Protection-33360bfb014e80f2aadbf151931873bb?pvs=21), Water
  Security & Watershed Protection (https://www.notion.so/Water-Security-Watershed-Protection-31260bfb014e8055bf4ee2a894cc45cd?pvs=21)
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Existence of community-managed Water User Associations (WUAs) with a dedicated budget and a formal 'Watershed Management Plan' recognized by local government authorities.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which water quality at community intake points improves through reductions in key contaminants associated with poor sanitation, unsustainable land use, and watershed degradation. This indicates the effectiveness of watershed protection and sanitation measures in safeguarding clean water sources.
Sub-themes:
Water Security & Watershed Protection (https://www.notion.so/Water-Security-Watershed-Protection-33360bfb014e80f2aadbf151931873bb?pvs=21), Water
  Security & Watershed Protection (https://www.notion.so/Water-Security-Watershed-Protection-31260bfb014e8055bf4ee2a894cc45cd?pvs=21)
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Proportion of households (disaggregated by gender and location) reporting a reduction in time spent fetching water and an increase in the reliability of water access during the dry season.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of households that experience easier and more reliable access to water during the dry season, reflected in less time spent collecting water and improved consistency of supply. Disaggregation by gender and location helps assess whether benefits are equitably distributed across different groups and areas.
Sub-themes:
Water Security & Watershed Protection (https://www.notion.so/Water-Security-Watershed-Protection-33360bfb014e80f2aadbf151931873bb?pvs=21), Water
  Security & Watershed Protection (https://www.notion.so/Water-Security-Watershed-Protection-31260bfb014e8055bf4ee2a894cc45cd?pvs=21)
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods
