Indicator statement: Percentage reduction in water-borne contaminants (e.g., sediment, nitrates, bacteria) at community intake points, resulting from the elimination of open-defecation, agricultural runoff, and erosion in protected watershed zones.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether functional community-managed Water User Associations are established and supported by both financial resources and a formally recognized watershed management plan. This reflects the strength of local governance and institutional capacity for sustainable water resource management.
Sub-themes:
Water Security & Watershed Protection (https://www.notion.so/Water-Security-Watershed-Protection-33360bfb014e80f2aadbf151931873bb?pvs=21), Water
  Security & Watershed Protection (https://www.notion.so/Water-Security-Watershed-Protection-31260bfb014e8055bf4ee2a894cc45cd?pvs=21)
Theme: Improved Ecosystem Services & Practices for Sustainable Livelihoods

Indicator statement: Percentage of project-related governing bodies (e.g., steering committees, trust boards) that meet a 40% minimum quota for women and youth representation, with clearly documented roles and responsibilities available to all community members within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Governance structures responsible for project-related decision-making are inclusive of all key community groups (Indigenous Peoples, women, youth, and persons with disabilities), have clearly defined roles and responsibilities, and function transparently and accountably.
Sub-themes:
Inclusive
  community governance structures (https://www.notion.so/Inclusive-community-governance-structures-31260bfb014e8046be97e86e16af9412?pvs=21), Inclusive community governance structures (https://www.notion.so/Inclusive-community-governance-structures-33360bfb014e808d95c7debf6005ce84?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members (disaggregated by Indigenous status, gender, and disability) who perceive project decision-making structures as inclusive, representative, and responsive to their group's specific needs within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Community governance structures responsible for project-related decision-making are perceiveed as being inclusive of all key community groups.
Sub-themes:
Inclusive
  community governance structures (https://www.notion.so/Inclusive-community-governance-structures-31260bfb014e8046be97e86e16af9412?pvs=21), Inclusive community governance structures (https://www.notion.so/Inclusive-community-governance-structures-33360bfb014e808d95c7debf6005ce84?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Percentage of community-led proposals or project modifications (disaggregated by IP and LC groups) that are formally integrated into the Project Management Plan or Annual Work Plan within <<specified period>>
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities have genuine opportunities to participate in decision-making processes related to project activities. This includes whether their voices are heard, their input is acted upon, and they feel they have real influence over project outcomes.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community-led proposals or project modifications (disaggregated by IP and LC groups) that are formally integrated into the Project Management Plan or Annual Work Plan within <<specified period>>
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities have genuine opportunities to participate in decision-making processes related to project activities. This includes whether their voices are heard, their input is acted upon, and they feel they have real influence over project outcomes.

Indicator statement: Percentage of executive leadership or officer positions within project governance bodies held by women, youth, and Indigenous persons, with a documented 100% participation rate in all budget-approval and policy-setting votes within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which marginalized groups are participate in governance structures. This includes tracking the composition of leadership positions, the meaningful influence of diverse leaders, and whether leadership is equitably distributed across gender, age, and Indigenous identity.

Indicator statement: Percentage of executive leadership or officer positions within project governance bodies held by women, youth, and Indigenous persons, with a documented 100% participation rate in all budget-approval and policy-setting votes within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which marginalized groups are participate in governance structures. This includes tracking the composition of leadership positions, the meaningful influence of diverse leaders, and whether leadership is equitably distributed across gender, age, and Indigenous identity.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members (disaggregated by gender and Indigenous status) who can correctly identify their legal rights regarding land tenure, FPIC, and service access, as well as their responsibilities toward collective resource management, within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether individuals understand their rights (e.g., to vote, express opinions, seek services) and responsibilities (e.g., participate in public life, respect others’ rights).

Indicator statement: Percentage of participants who report that their input during community forums resulted in a documented change in project activity or local policy, and who rate decision-maker accountability as 'high' within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether people feel their input leads to action and that decision-makers are accountable.

Indicator statement: Proportion of marginalized community members who have actively engaged in at least three participatory processes—such as community meetings, advocacy forums, or local elections—within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks whether individuals have engaged in community meetings, advocacy forums, local elections, or other participatory processes.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members (disaggregated by gender and Indigenous status) who can correctly identify their legal rights regarding land tenure, FPIC, and service access, as well as their responsibilities toward collective resource management, within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether individuals understand their rights (e.g., to vote, express opinions, seek services) and responsibilities (e.g., participate in public life, respect others’ rights).

Indicator statement: Percentage of community groups (including Indigenous and youth cohorts) actively engaged in the design of social monitoring indicators and the primary collection/analysis of field data within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which community members or local groups have actively participated in designing what is monitored, collecting and analysing data, and reflecting on progress and outcomes.
Sub-themes: Community-Led Learning, M&E, and Knowledge Sharing (https://www.notion.so/Community-Led-Learning-M-E-and-Knowledge-Sharing-33360bfb014e8053b358c091ecfb3b50?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members who report that monitoring data and reflection sessions have directly led to tangible improvements in project decision-making or activity implementation within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether community members feel that monitoring activities lead to improved decision-making, learning, or changes in project implementation.
Sub-themes: Community-Led Learning, M&E, and Knowledge Sharing (https://www.notion.so/Community-Led-Learning-M-E-and-Knowledge-Sharing-33360bfb014e8053b358c091ecfb3b50?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Percentage of individuals (disaggregated by gender and youth) reporting high levels of confidence and sufficient knowledge to make independent decisions regarding household livelihoods and land use within <<specified period>>
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether individuals feel they have the knowledge and confidence to make decisions related to livelihoods, land, services, or family well-being.

Indicator statement: Proportion of marginalized community members who have completed targeted capacity-building sessions (workshops, dialogues, or peer-learning) designed to strengthen negotiation and strategic planning skills within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which individuals have participated in training, workshops, or dialogue sessions that strengthen decision-making capacity.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members who report they can access timely, understandable, and trusted information (in local languages/formats) to guide decisions on project activities or legal rights within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks whether community members can access timely, understandable, and trusted information to guide decisions.

Indicator statement: Proportion of local leaders, traditional authorities, and community-based organizations (CBOs) that have completed certified training in governance, advocacy, and fiduciary management.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of local leaders, institutions, and community groups who have received training in governance, advocacy, and leadership skills.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members who rate local leadership structures as 'fair, inclusive, and effective' in their management of project resources and conflict resolution within <<specified period>>.
Indicator descriptor: Measures how community members view the inclusiveness, fairness, and effectiveness of local leadership structures.

Indicator statement: Percentage of trained leaders who demonstrate improved confidence scores in facilitating inclusive, transparent decision-making and managing project-related technical tasks.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether trained leaders feel confident and equipped to lead and make inclusive, transparent decisions.

Indicator statement: Percentage change in natural forest cover and rate of land degradation within the project boundary compared to the historical baseline and a matched proxy 'control' area.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the effectiveness of project interventions in preventing deforestation, land degradation, and illegal land conversion by tracking changes in forest cover over time.

Indicator statement: Percentage of households within the project boundary participating in active stewardship roles (e.g., community forest patrols, seed collection, nursery management, or assisted natural regeneration) and the total area (hectares) managed under these community-led initiatives.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities actively participate in forest protection, conservation, and restoration activities, and their perception of the effectiveness of these efforts in preventing deforestation and environmental degradation.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who perceive that project-supported restoration and protection activities have successfully reduced deforestation and improved ecosystem health compared to the pre-project baseline.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of community members living within the project boundary who perceive that project-supported restoration and protection activities have led to a reduction in deforestation and an improvement in overall ecosystem health compared to conditions before the project began. This reflects community-level perceptions of environmental change and the perceived effectiveness of project interventions.

Indicator statement: Percentage of forest management activities within the project boundary (e.g., carbon stock inventory, firebreak maintenance, and nursery operations) conducted independently by community groups using locally managed infrastructure and skills.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which IPLCs have the necessary skills, knowledge, infrastructure, and financial resources to effectively manage forests without external dependency.

Indicator statement: Inventory of project-essential infrastructure (e.g., monitoring equipment, vehicles, and processing facilities) within the project boundary for which legal ownership and maintenance responsibilities have been transferred to local community institutions."
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which IPLCs have the necessary skills, knowledge, infrastructure, and financial resources to effectively manage forests without external dependency.

Indicator statement: Proportion of forest management costs within the project boundary covered by community-managed funds (e.g., carbon revenue dividends or sustainable forest enterprise profits) rather than external donor grants.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which IPLCs have the necessary skills, knowledge, infrastructure, and financial resources to effectively manage forests without external dependency.

Indicator statement: Number of recorded attempts or actual instances of illegal land conversion (e.g., unauthorized mining, logging, or agricultural expansion) within the project boundary detected and successfully mitigated through community-led enforcement.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the effectiveness of project interventions in preventing deforestation, land degradation, and illegal land conversion by tracking changes in forest cover over time.

Indicator statement: Number of community-led monitoring patrols and the frequency of rapid-response actions taken to mitigate encroachment threats—such as illegal logging, land grabbing, or wildfires—detected within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the effectiveness of community-led strategies in preventing encroachment-related threats such as illegal logging, land grabbing, and wildfires. This tracks community engagement, enforcement measures, and perceived success in maintaining forest integrity.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members within the project boundary actively engaged in the project’s ‘Social Fence’—including participation in land-rights advocacy, boundary marking, and the reporting of unauthorized land-use changes.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the effectiveness of community-led strategies in preventing encroachment-related threats such as illegal logging, land grabbing, and wildfires. This tracks community engagement, enforcement measures, and perceived success in maintaining forest integrity.

Indicator statement: Percentage of households within the project boundary who rate the community’s collective ability to prevent outside threats (fire, logging, and land-grabbing) as 'high' and report a felt sense of security over their forest resources.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the effectiveness of community-led strategies in preventing encroachment-related threats such as illegal logging, land grabbing, and wildfires. This tracks community engagement, enforcement measures, and perceived success in maintaining forest integrity.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members within the project boundary trained in fire-line construction, early warning systems, and controlled burning techniques, and the proportion of high-risk zones actively managed under a disaster risk reduction plan.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the level of preparedness, training, and response mechanisms within communities to prevent and mitigate wildfires.

Indicator statement: Number of functional fire-response units established within the project boundary equipped with necessary infrastructure (e.g., communications gear, water points) and their documented response time to detected fire alerts.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the level of preparedness, training, and response mechanisms within communities to prevent and mitigate wildfires.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members within the project boundary (disaggregated by gender and age) who can accurately describe local, national, and international forest protection laws and their specific legal rights regarding land tenure and carbon ownership.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities are aware of local, national, or international forest protection laws and regulations.

Indicator statement: Percentage of households within the project boundary who report that forest protection policies are enforced fairly, consistently, and effectively by both local institutions and state authorities.
Indicator descriptor: Community perceptions about the effectiveness of the enforcement of forest protection policies

Indicator statement: Percentage of households within the project boundary deriving a significant portion of their annual income or subsistence from project-supported sustainable forest-based activities (e.g., agroforestry, certified Non-Timber Forest Product [NTFP] harvesting, or ecotourism)
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities are engaged in sustainable forest-based economic activities (e.g., agroforestry, non-timber forest product harvesting, ecotourism).

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who report a belief that their forest-based livelihoods are directly dependent on, and contribute to, the long-term maintenance of forest integrity.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities believe that sustainable forest-based livelihoods help maintain forest integrity.

Indicator statement: Percentage of households within the project boundary reporting improved year-round availability and observable quality (clarity, taste, and safety) of water from local forest-fed sources compared to the project baseline.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities have seen improved water quality and availability.

Indicator statement: Number of community-led initiatives within the project boundary specifically focused on riparian zone restoration (e.g., planting along riverbanks) and the protection of headwaters or local springs from livestock or agricultural runoff.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities have seen improved water quality and availability.

Indicator statement: Percentage of project staff (disaggregated by gender, ethnicity, and age) within the project boundary who report that compensation is equitable, transparent, and free from discrimination compared to peers in similar roles.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which project staff perceive that compensation is equitable and free from discrimination.

Indicator statement: Documented existence of a standardized salary scale and benefit package that meets or exceeds the national living wage and is applied consistently to all project personnel working within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: Evidence of equitable and fair pay

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members and project workers within the project boundary who report being treated with dignity and fairness, with zero recorded instances of discrimination based on gender, ethnicity, disability, age, or religion.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which all individuals involved in the project—whether workers, community members, or stakeholders—are treated fairly and equitably, without discrimination based on gender, ethnicity, disability, age, religion, or other protected characteristics.

Indicator statement: Number of project roles filled through a documented, transparent, and non-discriminatory hiring process, and the percentage of project staff recruited from diverse or marginalized groups within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which hiring processes within the project are transparent, merit-based, and free from discrimination. The indicator evaluates adherence to non-discriminatory policies, the representation of diverse groups in hiring outcomes, and the perception of fairness in employment opportunities.

Indicator statement: Percentage of local residents within the project boundary who perceive that employment and training opportunities provided by the project are accessible to all community members regardless of their social or economic background.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which hiring processes within the project are transparent, merit-based, and free from discrimination. The indicator evaluates adherence to non-discriminatory policies, the representation of diverse groups in hiring outcomes, and the perception of fairness in employment opportunities.

Indicator statement: Number of reported incidents or 'red flag' alerts related to child labor, forced labor, or exploitation detected through the project’s internal monitoring or grievance mechanism, and the documented speed and effectiveness of the remediation process.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks the number of reported incidents related to child labor or forced labour
Sub-themes: Prevention of Forced Labour, Child Labour, Trafficking, and Exploitation (https://www.notion.so/Prevention-of-Forced-Labour-Child-Labour-Trafficking-and-Exploitation-33360bfb014e805aaf3dcc63860b7eba?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Percentage of project-managed sites and contracted entities within the project boundary that have undergone formal age-verification audits and possess signed 'Zero Tolerance' agreements regarding forced labor and human trafficking.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which the project has established and implemented safeguards to prevent forced and child labour
Sub-themes: Prevention of Forced Labour, Child Labour, Trafficking, and Exploitation (https://www.notion.so/Prevention-of-Forced-Labour-Child-Labour-Trafficking-and-Exploitation-33360bfb014e805aaf3dcc63860b7eba?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Presence of a formal 'Code of Conduct' signed by all project personnel, the percentage of staff and community members trained in GBV prevention, and the existence of safe, gender-segregated facilities (e.g., separate housing and sanitation) at project work sites.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which clear policies, awareness training, workplace safeguards, and community-based interventions are in place to prevent sexual harassment and gender-based violence (GBV) within the project.
Sub-themes: Prevention of Sexual Harassment & Gender-Based Violence (https://www.notion.so/Prevention-of-Sexual-Harassment-Gender-Based-Violence-33360bfb014e805f95b3f69868b137c2?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Number of reported GBV or harassment incidents and the percentage of those cases managed through a survivor-centric protocol that ensures 100% confidentiality, immediate medical/psychological referral, and documented accountability for the perpetrator.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks the number of reported incidents of sexual harassment and gender-based violence (GBV) within the project, as well as the responses taken to address them. This includes monitoring the frequency of reports, the types of cases reported, the timeliness of responses, and the outcomes of investigations. The indicator also assesses whether incidents are handled in a way that ensures survivor protection, confidentiality, and accountability
Sub-themes: Prevention of Sexual Harassment & Gender-Based Violence (https://www.notion.so/Prevention-of-Sexual-Harassment-Gender-Based-Violence-33360bfb014e805f95b3f69868b137c2?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Percentage of project worksites within the project boundary that meet international OHS standards (e.g., ISO 45001 or equivalent), characterized by the 100% availability of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), first-aid kits, and documented safety protocols.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which workplace health and safety standards are implemented and followed across all project sites.

Indicator statement: Percentage of project personnel within the project boundary who have completed mandatory health and safety training and can demonstrate correct emergency procedures, including the use of communication devices in the event of an accident.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which workplace health and safety standards are implemented and followed across all project sites.

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who express a high level of trust in their neighbors to adhere to collective forest-management agreements and act fairly in the distribution of project-related benefits.
Indicator descriptor: Measures how much individuals trust others in their community to act fairly, respectfully, and with good intentions.
Sub-themes: Bonding Social Capital: Trust and Support Within Communities (https://www.notion.so/Bonding-Social-Capital-Trust-and-Support-Within-Communities-31260bfb014e808790a3d696773c94e6?pvs=21)
Theme: Social Cohesion and Strengthened Social Capital

Indicator statement: Index score of 'Sense of Belonging' among project participants, measuring the extent to which individuals identify with the community’s environmental goals and feel an emotional connection to the shared heritage of the landscape within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: Captures emotional connection and identification with the community — essential for cohesion.
Sub-themes: Bonding Social Capital: Trust and Support Within Communities (https://www.notion.so/Bonding-Social-Capital-Trust-and-Support-Within-Communities-31260bfb014e808790a3d696773c94e6?pvs=21)
Theme: Social Cohesion and Strengthened Social Capital

Indicator statement: Frequency and diversity of collaborative activities (e.g., joint forest patrols, multi-village water committees, or inter-clan nurseries) involving individuals from different social, ethnic, or geographic backgrounds within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which people from different backgrounds engage with one another and work together.
Sub-themes: Bridging Social Capital: Collaboration Across Groups (https://www.notion.so/Bridging-Social-Capital-Collaboration-Across-Groups-31260bfb014e803ab0a7d28d257db237?pvs=21)
Theme: Social Cohesion and Strengthened Social Capital

Indicator statement: Percentage of individuals from marginalized or 'outsider' groups within the project boundary who report an increase in trust toward members of the dominant social group and a perceived reduction in social barriers to project participation.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether divisions between social groups are shrinking and trust is improving.
Sub-themes: Bridging Social Capital: Collaboration Across Groups (https://www.notion.so/Bridging-Social-Capital-Collaboration-Across-Groups-31260bfb014e803ab0a7d28d257db237?pvs=21)
Theme: Social Cohesion and Strengthened Social Capital

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who express 'high confidence' in the group’s ability to successfully organize and resolve local environmental or social challenges (e.g., managing water shortages or preventing illegal encroachment).
Indicator descriptor: Measures how confident individuals are that their community can work together to address challenges.
Theme: Social Cohesion and Strengthened Social Capital

Indicator statement: Percentage of households within the project boundary who report that they can rely on fellow community members for practical, emotional, or material support (e.g., labor sharing, food, or emergency funds) during times of crisis or hardship.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether people feel they can rely on others in their community for emotional, practical, or material support during crises or hardship.
Theme: Social Cohesion and Strengthened Social Capital

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who express trust in the project implementers and traditional authorities to manage carbon revenues fairly and act in the community's long-term best interest.
Indicator descriptor: Measures how much community members trust institutions (e.g. project implementers, NGOs, traditional authorities, and government) to act fairly and in their best interest.
Sub-themes: Linking Social Capital: Trust in Institutions and External Actors (https://www.notion.so/Linking-Social-Capital-Trust-in-Institutions-and-External-Actors-31260bfb014e80218c3decbc5194f8d4?pvs=21)
Theme: Social Cohesion and Strengthened Social Capital

Indicator statement: Level of explicit trust expressed by community members toward the project developer, specifically regarding the transparency of carbon credit sales and the reliability of long-term partnership commitments within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: Degree to which community members express confidence that the project developer shares clear, accurate, and timely information about carbon credit sales, benefit flows, and related decisions, and will honor agreed long-term responsibilities, relationships, and commitments within the project area.
Sub-themes: Linking Social Capital: Trust in Institutions and External Actors (https://www.notion.so/Linking-Social-Capital-Trust-in-Institutions-and-External-Actors-31260bfb014e80218c3decbc5194f8d4?pvs=21)
Theme: Social Cohesion and Strengthened Social Capital

Indicator statement: Percentage of community members within the project boundary who agree that external project actors actively listen to their concerns, provide transparent information, and have demonstrably changed project activities based on community feedback.
Indicator descriptor: Assesses whether communities feel that external actors listen to their concerns, provide information, and take action based on feedback.
Sub-themes: Linking Social Capital: Trust in Institutions and External Actors (https://www.notion.so/Linking-Social-Capital-Trust-in-Institutions-and-External-Actors-31260bfb014e80218c3decbc5194f8d4?pvs=21)
Theme: Social Cohesion and Strengthened Social Capital

Indicator statement: Number of community members, local staff, and government officials who have completed training on carbon credit lifecycles, pricing mechanisms, and the roles of registries and brokers.
Indicator descriptor: Extent of stakeholder exposure to structured training on carbon market systems, including the full carbon credit lifecycle, pricing dynamics, and the functions of key market actors such as registries and brokers.

Indicator statement: Percentage of local technical staff and community 'carbon champions' who can independently perform biomass measurements, use digital monitoring tools, and explain the concept of 'additionality' and 'leakage' within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: Level of practical technical capacity among local actors to independently carry out carbon measurement, monitoring, and reporting tasks, and to demonstrate applied understanding of core carbon accounting principles such as additionality and leakage.

Indicator statement: Total number and percentage of community members (disaggregated by gender, age, and marginalized status) within the project boundary who have successfully attended and completed certified training modules related to project activities.
Indicator descriptor: The number of community members who have attended and completed training programmes.

Indicator statement: Number of distinct technical and administrative 'learning pathways' completed by community members (e.g., nursery management, carbon monitoring, financial bookkeeping, or fire response) to ensure a diversified skill set within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: The number of community members who have attended and completed training programmes.

Indicator statement: Total number and designation of government officials (at local, regional, or national levels) who have successfully completed specialized training modules designed to align project activities with national forest policies and carbon accounting standards.
Indicator descriptor: The number of Government staff who have attended and completed training programmes.

Indicator statement: Number of joint training sessions conducted between project developers and government agencies focused on legal compliance, anti-corruption safeguards, and the integration of the project's social safeguards into local administrative frameworks.
Indicator descriptor: The number of Government staff who have attended and completed training programmes.

Indicator statement: Total number and percentage of partner organization staff (e.g., local NGOs or technical consultants) who have successfully completed training on the project's specific Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), safeguarding policies, and MRV protocols.
Indicator descriptor: The number of partners who have attended and completed training programmes.

Indicator statement: Number of partner staff trained in specialized technical domains required for project success, such as remote sensing analysis, gender-sensitive social surveying, or biodiversity monitoring techniques specific to the project's ecosystem.
Indicator descriptor: The number of partners who have attended and completed training programmes.

Indicator statement: Total number and percentage of local project staff and contracted workers (disaggregated by role and gender) who have successfully completed mandatory induction and specialized training programs required for their specific duties within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: The number of local staff and contracted workers who have attended and completed training programmes.

Indicator statement: Percentage of field-based staff and contractors who have completed training on the project's 'Code of Conduct,' including non-discrimination, anti-harassment, and the Prevention of Sexual Harassment & GBV, before commencing work within the project boundary.
Indicator descriptor: The number of local staff and contracted workers who have attended and completed training programmes.

Indicator statement: Existence of formal accountability structures for project decision-making
Indicator descriptor: Existence of formal accountability structures for project decision-making

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members who are aware of at least one grievance or feedback mechanism, can access it without barriers, and report confidence that concerns will be handled fairly and independently.
Indicator descriptor: Establishing accessible and independent channels for resolving community concerns.

Indicator statement: Proportion of stakeholders who report that project information is regularly disclosed, easy to access, understandable, and available in appropriate formats and local languages.
Indicator descriptor: The transparency of project disclosures and the accessibility of information to stakeholders

Indicator statement: Proportion of Indigenous Peoples and Local Community members who report awareness of at least three key types of publicly available project information (e.g., project objectives, carbon credit revenues, benefit-sharing arrangements, safeguards) within the last 12 months.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities are aware of publicly available project information

Indicator statement: Extent to which carbon credit pricing, sales revenues, and distribution formulas are disclosed to communities.
Indicator descriptor: Degree to which communities receive clear, complete, and timely information about carbon credit pricing, total sales revenues, and the formulas used to allocate and distribute benefits, in formats that are understandable, accessible, and verifiable.

Indicator statement: Proportion of IPLCs that have secure, legally recognized, and enforceable rights to access, use, manage, and benefit from land and natural resources. This includes formalizing land tenure, preventing land grabbing and displacement, and promoting equitable land governance. Strengthening user rights ensures that IPLCs can make decisions over their land, maintain their livelihoods, and participate in conservation and development initiatives without the risk of exclusion or loss of access
Indicator descriptor: Ensuring that Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities (IPLCs) have secure, legally recognized, and enforceable rights to access, use, manage, and benefit from land and natural resources. This includes formalizing land tenure, preventing land grabbing and displacement, and promoting equitable land governance. Strengthening user rights ensures that IPLCs can make decisions over their land, maintain their livelihoods, and participate in conservation and development initiatives without the risk of exclusion or loss of access

Indicator statement: Proportion pof project beneficiaries who have access to  carbon rights that are legally defined, recognized, and attributed to IPLCs in project jurisdiction.
Indicator descriptor: Extent to which carbon rights are legally defined, recognized, and attributed to IPLCs in project jurisdiction.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members (disaggregated by gender, age, Indigenous status, and role in community) who perceive that their cultural heritage, traditional knowledge, and land stewardship practices have been respected and meaningfully incorporated into project design and implementation.
Indicator descriptor: The extent to which Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities (IPLCs) report that their traditional knowledge, land stewardship practices, and cultural heritage were respected and integrated into project activities.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members are aware of their land tenure rights and related legal processes
Indicator descriptor: Extent to which community members are aware of their land tenure rights and related legal processes

Indicator statement: Percentage of traditional lands and territories used or occupied by IPLCs that are formally recognized or owned by them.
Indicator descriptor: Extent to which formal land rights for Indigenous Peoples and Local Communities (IPLCs) are secure, ensuring long-term security and reducing land-related conflicts.

Indicator statement: Proportion of IPLC community members and relevant external stakeholders who report no involuntary displacement or new restrictive land-use changes attributable to the project in the last [specify period, e.g., 12 months].
Indicator descriptor: Extent to which IPLCs and stakeholders confirm that project activities have not resulted in involuntary displacement or restrictions on land use
Sub-themes: Land use plans agreed – no displacement (https://www.notion.so/Land-use-plans-agreed-no-displacement-33360bfb014e801ca76bf2fd14661cf1?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Land use plans comprehensively describe and map statutory and customary tenure/use/access/management rights, including individual, collective, overlapping, and conflicting rights.
Indicator descriptor: Land use plans comprehensively describe and map statutory and customary tenure/use/access/management rights, including individual, collective, overlapping, and conflicting rights.
Sub-themes: Land use plans agreed – no displacement (https://www.notion.so/Land-use-plans-agreed-no-displacement-33360bfb014e801ca76bf2fd14661cf1?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of surveyed IPLC community members (disaggregated by gender, age, and subgroups) who report satisfaction with the inclusiveness and participation in the land-use planning process, m
Indicator descriptor: Extent to which community members report that land use plans were developed through an inclusive and participatory process
Sub-themes: Land use plans agreed – no displacement (https://www.notion.so/Land-use-plans-agreed-no-displacement-33360bfb014e801ca76bf2fd14661cf1?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of IPLC community members that confirm they can continue traditional resource use within project areas.
Indicator descriptor: Extent to which IPLCs confirm they can continue traditional resource use within project areas.
Sub-themes: Land use plans agreed – no displacement (https://www.notion.so/Land-use-plans-agreed-no-displacement-33360bfb014e801ca76bf2fd14661cf1?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who report taking at least one verifiable goal-directed action toward a stated education or livelihood goal in the 6 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who can report a specific, nameable action taken toward a defined education or livelihood goal within a 6-month recall window, used as a behavioural proxy for goal-directed agency and future orientation.

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who can name and describe at least two identity markers that are meaningful to them, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of learners who can cognitively recognise and articulate identity markers relevant to their social and cultural context, used as a proxy for identity awareness and reflective self-concept.

Indicator statement: Average number of school-organised events or meetings attended by caregivers per school term, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the mean frequency of caregiver attendance at school-organised events or meetings per term, used as a proxy for sustained caregiver participation in the school community beyond minimum threshold presence.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who attended at least one school-organised event or meeting in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report physical attendance at a school-organised event or meeting, used as a minimum threshold indicator of caregiver presence in the school community.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report feeling welcomed and respected when engaging with school staff, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report a positive experience of reception and respect when interacting with school staff, used as a proxy for the relational quality of the school environment as experienced by caregivers.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report volunteering time or skills at the school at least once in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report contributing voluntary time or skills to school activities beyond mandatory attendance, used as a proxy for active and self-initiated caregiver participation in the school community.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report sitting with their child during homework or study at least three times per week in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report regularly sitting with and supporting their child during homework or study sessions, used as a behavioural proxy for active home-based learning support.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Support for Learning at Home (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Support-for-Learning-at-Home-33e60bfb014e814aabb4df57c5750cbf?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report reading aloud to or with their child, including oral storytelling, at least once per week in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report reading aloud to or with their child at least once per week, including oral storytelling and informal reading materials, used as a proxy for caregiver oral literacy engagement in the home learning environment.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Support for Learning at Home (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Support-for-Learning-at-Home-33e60bfb014e814aabb4df57c5750cbf?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report both monitoring their child's daily school attendance and following up on absences in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report both monitoring their child's daily school attendance and following up when their child is absent, used as a proxy for active caregiver responsibility for school attendance as a home-based support behaviour.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Support for Learning at Home (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Support-for-Learning-at-Home-33e60bfb014e814aabb4df57c5750cbf?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report their child has access to a designated, adequately lit, and reasonably quiet space for homework or study at home, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report that their child has access to a dedicated, adequately lit, and reasonably quiet space for study at home, used as a structural proxy for the physical conditions of the home learning environment.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Support for Learning at Home (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Support-for-Learning-at-Home-33e60bfb014e814aabb4df57c5750cbf?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report a strong belief in the value of education for their child's future, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report a strong positive belief in the value of education for their child's future, used as a proxy for the attitudinal foundation underlying sustained caregiver engagement with learning.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Attitudes and Beliefs about Education (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Attitudes-and-Beliefs-about-Education-33e60bfb014e818db731ea80713f3b27?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report aspirations of secondary completion or above as the highest level of education they hope their child will achieve, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who aspire to secondary completion or above as the highest level of education they hope their child will achieve, used as a proxy for caregiver educational aspirations and their role as a motivator of learner persistence.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Attitudes and Beliefs about Education (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Attitudes-and-Beliefs-about-Education-33e60bfb014e818db731ea80713f3b27?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report a strong belief in their personal role as an active contributor to their child's learning, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report a strong belief in their own active role as a contributor to their child's learning, used as a proxy for caregiver role construction and its function as a mediator between attitudes and engagement behaviours.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Attitudes and Beliefs about Education (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Attitudes-and-Beliefs-about-Education-33e60bfb014e818db731ea80713f3b27?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report high confidence in their ability to support their child with schoolwork across defined learning support tasks, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report high confidence in their ability to support their child with schoolwork across defined learning support tasks, used as a proxy for caregiver self-efficacy as a predictor of home-based engagement behaviour.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Confidence and Capacity to Engage (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Confidence-and-Capacity-to-Engage-33e60bfb014e81af98f9cdae9bb69228?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report high confidence in approaching school staff, asking questions about their child's progress, and raising concerns, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report high confidence in their ability to approach school staff, ask questions about their child's progress, and raise concerns, used as a proxy for caregiver communicative confidence as a precondition for school-based engagement.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Confidence and Capacity to Engage (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Confidence-and-Capacity-to-Engage-33e60bfb014e81af98f9cdae9bb69228?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report functional literacy and numeracy capacity sufficient to support their child's learning at home, assessed through self-report and an optional functional task at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who self-assess as able to read school communications, understand a report card, and support basic reading or numeracy tasks at home, used as a proxy for functional literacy and numeracy capacity as a structural condition for home-based learning support.
Sub-themes: Caregiver Confidence and Capacity to Engage (https://www.notion.so/Caregiver-Confidence-and-Capacity-to-Engage-33e60bfb014e81af98f9cdae9bb69228?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report cultural norms or gender-based expectations as preventing or limiting their engagement with school, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report cultural norms, gender roles, or social expectations as preventing or limiting their engagement with school, used as a diagnostic equity indicator for identifying norm-based barriers to caregiver participation.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report language or communication barriers preventing or limiting their ability to engage with school communications, meetings, or homework support, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report language or communication barriers as preventing or limiting their ability to understand school communications, participate in school meetings, or support homework, used as a diagnostic equity indicator in multilingual programme contexts.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report at least one structural or logistical barrier preventing or significantly limiting their engagement with school, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report at least one structural or logistical barrier, such as work schedule, distance, or transport cost, as preventing or significantly limiting their engagement with their child's school, used as a diagnostic indicator for contextualising low engagement rates.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report applying at least one programme-learned strategy or approach at home with their child since their last programme session, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report trying at least one specific strategy or approach learned in a programme session at home with their child since the last session attended, used as a behaviour transfer indicator linking adult learning participation to home practice.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who attended at least one structured facilitated group learning session in the programme reference period, verified through attendance records and caregiver self-report at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who attended at least one structured facilitated group learning session in the programme reference period, used as a process-level indicator of adult learning participation and a precondition for downstream empowerment outcomes.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report seeking or providing topic-specific peer support related to their child's learning or wellbeing in the three months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report seeking or providing topic-specific peer support related to their child's learning or wellbeing in the three months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for active participation in caregiver peer support networks.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who demonstrate at least a 20 percent improvement in knowledge across programme-defined child development and learning support domains between baseline and the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who demonstrate a defined improvement in knowledge across programme-defined child development and learning support domains between baseline and the time of survey, used as a learning outcome indicator for adult education participation.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report a strong sense of peer belonging and mutual support within their programme group, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report a strong sense of belonging, mutual understanding, and perceived peer support within their programme group, used as a proxy for group cohesion as a predictor of sustained adult learning participation and individual empowerment outcomes.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who attended at least three peer support gatherings in the six months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who attended at least three peer support gatherings in the six months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for sustained participation in caregiver peer networks as distinct from structured curriculum-based learning.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report a strong sense of personal agency in influencing decisions about their child's education, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report a strong sense of personal agency in influencing decisions about their child's education, used as a proxy for caregiver empowerment as a rights-bearing and relational construct distinct from task-specific confidence.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report a strong sense of collective empowerment and shared capacity to influence their school or community environment through programme participation, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report a strong sense of collective efficacy and shared capacity to influence their school or community environment through programme participation, used as a proxy for collective empowerment as a group-level outcome distinct from individual agency.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report meaningful input into at least three of four defined household decisions affecting their child's educational participation, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report meaningful input into at least three of four defined household decisions affecting their child's educational participation, used as a proxy for caregiver household decision-making power as a determinant of consistent learning participation.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report completing all three steps of advocacy, identifying a concern, communicating it formally, and following up, in the six months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report identifying a learning or wellbeing concern, communicating it formally to a teacher or school authority, and following up on the response, in the six months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for caregiver advocacy capacity as a higher-order empowerment outcome.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who report having at least one reliable source of both practical and emotional support when facing caregiving or household challenges, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who report having at least one reliable source of both practical and emotional support when facing caregiving or household challenges, used as a proxy for social support availability as a structural wellbeing resource and buffer against caregiver stress and burnout.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who score at or above the adequacy threshold on a validated brief hope scale covering agency and pathway thinking, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who score at or above a defined adequacy threshold on a validated brief hope scale covering agency and pathway thinking, used as a proxy for caregiver hope orientation as a motivational wellbeing construct and predictor of sustained engagement and educational aspiration.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who score above the high-stress threshold on a validated brief perceived stress screening tool, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who score above a defined threshold on a validated brief perceived stress screening tool, used as a diagnostic upstream indicator of caregiving quality and a contextual baseline for interpreting all other caregiver engagement indicators.

Indicator statement: Proportion of caregivers who score at or above the adequacy threshold on a validated brief resilience scale, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of caregivers who score at or above a defined adequacy threshold on a validated brief resilience scale, used as a proxy for caregiver capacity to maintain consistent caregiving quality under conditions of adversity and household vulnerability.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members who report being offered at least one structured opportunity to contribute input to programme design or review in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of community members who report being offered at least one structured opportunity to contribute input to programme design or review in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a coverage indicator of participation access across the community.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members who participated in a programme consultation or review and report a meaningful participation experience, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of community members who participated in a programme consultation or review and report that their input was genuinely heard, taken seriously, and considered in programme decisions, used as a proxy for the perceived quality and accountability of community participation.

Indicator statement: Proportion of closed community feedback cases where the originating community member confirms satisfactory resolution, in the reporting period
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of administratively closed community feedback cases where the originating community member confirms that their concern was resolved to their satisfaction, used as a community-verified resolution quality indicator for the programme feedback and accountability mechanism.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme partnerships that meet all three qualifying criteria, a signed agreement, documented roles and responsibilities, and an evidenced active review arrangement, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of programme partnerships that meet all three qualifying governance criteria, a signed agreement, documented roles and responsibilities, and an evidenced active review arrangement, used as a composite process indicator of partnership formalisation quality across the programme portfolio.

Indicator statement: Proportion of active partners who rate coordination as effective across four defined operational dimensions in the annual partnership review instrument
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of active partners who rate joint workplan alignment, resource allocation, delivery gap management, and joint decision-making timeliness as effective in the annual partnership review instrument, used as a proxy for operational coordination effectiveness across the programme partnership portfolio.

Indicator statement: Proportion of active partners who rate communication as effective across four defined relational and informational dimensions in the annual partnership review instrument
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of active partners who rate information timeliness, message clarity, relevance of information shared, and openness to raising concerns as effective in the annual partnership review instrument, used as a proxy for relational communication effectiveness across the programme partnership portfolio.

Indicator statement: Proportion of targeted decision-makers on the programme stakeholder map who participated in at least one structured programme-organised engagement event in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of decision-makers on the programme stakeholder map who participated in at least one structured programme-organised engagement event in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as an active engagement coverage rate for the programme's relational influencing strategy.

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who report participating in at least one voluntary or programme-supported community service or contribution activity in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who report participating in at least one voluntary or programme-supported community service or contribution activity in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for civic contribution and prosocial engagement beyond the immediate peer group.

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who can identify at least one instance where an idea or suggestion they contributed was adopted or acted upon in a group, school, or community setting in the six months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who can identify at least one instance where an idea or suggestion they contributed was adopted or acted upon in a group, school, or community setting in the six months prior to the survey, used as an evidenced influence indicator among youth with documented voice access.

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who report raising a community concern or issue through a formal or structured civic channel in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who report raising a community concern or issue through a formal or structured civic channel in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for civic advocacy behaviour as a higher-order expression of active citizenship.

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who report feeling that their contribution and leadership are seen and valued by adults and institutions in their programme and community environment, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who report feeling that their contribution and leadership are seen and valued by adults and institutions in their programme and community environment, used as a perceptual recognition indicator complementing the formal recognition and visibility indicators.
Sub-themes: Recognition and Visibility of Youth Contribution (https://www.notion.so/Recognition-and-Visibility-of-Youth-Contribution-33e60bfb014e816ea1b4c9d55bf8e0d5?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who report a strong sense of civic agency, civic responsibility, and civic belonging, measured on a validated scale at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who report a strong sense of civic agency, civic responsibility, and civic belonging, used as a proxy for civic identity as the motivational and identity foundation for sustained active citizenship beyond programme participation.

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who report participating in at least one formal civic structure or process existing independently of the programme in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who report participating in at least one formal civic structure or process existing independently of the programme in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for active citizenship development beyond programme-structured settings.

Indicator statement: Equity gap in formal recognition rates across defined youth sub-groups, gender, age band, and community, assessed annually through disaggregated analysis of programme recognition data
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether formal recognition of youth contribution is equitably distributed across gender, age band, and community sub-group within the programme, used as a system-level equity diagnostic to identify structural bias in recognition practices that may reinforce marginalisation.
Sub-themes: Recognition and Visibility of Youth Contribution (https://www.notion.so/Recognition-and-Visibility-of-Youth-Contribution-33e60bfb014e816ea1b4c9d55bf8e0d5?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who can correctly identify at least three basic civic rights and at least one formal accountability mechanism available to them in their community, at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who can correctly identify at least three basic civic rights and at least one formal accountability mechanism available to them in their community, used as a proxy for foundational civic knowledge as a precondition for informed civic participation.

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth whose contribution or leadership was made publicly visible to the broader community beyond the immediate programme or peer group in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth whose contribution or leadership was made publicly visible to the broader community beyond the immediate programme or peer group in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for community visibility of youth contribution as a structural mechanism for sustaining collective investment in youth leadership.
Sub-themes: Recognition and Visibility of Youth Contribution (https://www.notion.so/Recognition-and-Visibility-of-Youth-Contribution-33e60bfb014e816ea1b4c9d55bf8e0d5?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of youth who report receiving explicit formal acknowledgement of their contribution or leadership from an adult, institution, or community figure in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of youth who report receiving explicit, documented acknowledgement of their contribution or leadership from an adult, institution, or community figure in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for formal recognition as a structural condition for sustained youth participation.
Sub-themes: Recognition and Visibility of Youth Contribution (https://www.notion.so/Recognition-and-Visibility-of-Youth-Contribution-33e60bfb014e816ea1b4c9d55bf8e0d5?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme partners who submitted their most recent monitoring report using the shared programme reporting template as the primary reporting structure, in the current reporting period
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of programme partners who submitted their most recent monitoring report using the shared programme reporting template as the primary reporting structure, used as a proxy for reporting format alignment across the partner portfolio.

Indicator statement: Proportion of convened joint learning or evidence review meetings that produced a documented follow-up action log with named responsible parties and target completion dates, in the programme year
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of convened joint learning or evidence review meetings that produced a documented follow-up action log meeting defined quality criteria, used as a proxy for the substantive productivity of collective learning practice across the programme and its partners.

Indicator statement: Proportion of shared data submissions that meet defined quality standards for completeness, consistency, and plausibility in the current reporting period
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of shared data submissions that meet defined quality standards for completeness, consistency, and plausibility in the current reporting period, used as a data quality indicator for the joint monitoring system's fitness for collective decision-making.

Indicator statement: Whether at least one collective programme decision traceable to shared monitoring data and resulting in a documented programme action occurred in the reporting period
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether at least one collective programme decision traceable to shared monitoring data and resulting in a documented programme action occurred in the reporting period, used as an outcome indicator for whether joint monitoring is translating into evidence-informed collective decision-making.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme staff and partner representatives who report embedded collective reflection practice across regularity, usefulness, and visible learning uptake dimensions, measured on a structured instrument at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of programme staff and partner representatives who report that structured collective reflection is regular, useful, and visibly influencing programme adaptation, used as a proxy for embedded collective reflection practice as a component of a learning culture.

Indicator statement: Proportion of active programme partners who confirm receipt and can recall at least one lesson from a programme-produced lesson-learned document in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of active programme partners who confirm receipt and recall at least one lesson from a programme-produced lesson-learned document in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for the reach and uptake of lesson documentation and sharing practice.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme partners required to share data who submitted all required data on time, with complete coverage, and in the agreed format in the current reporting period
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of programme partners required to share data who submitted all required data on time, with complete coverage, and in the agreed format in the current reporting period, used as a process fidelity indicator for the operational functionality of the joint monitoring data sharing system.

Indicator statement: Proportion of planned cross-sector referral pathways that are documented, known to frontline practitioners, and evidenced through at least one referral in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of planned cross-sector referral pathways that are documented, known to frontline practitioners, and evidenced through at least one referral in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for the functionality of cross-sector coordination infrastructure.

Indicator statement: Proportion of cross-sector referrals that received a documented acknowledgement or initial response within the protocol-specified timeframe in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of cross-sector referrals made through programme-linked pathways that received a documented acknowledgement or initial response within the protocol-specified timeframe in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for the operational timeliness of cross-sector referral systems.

Indicator statement: Proportion of frontline practitioners who report adequate awareness of, confidence in, and trust in cross-sector referral pathways, measured on a structured instrument at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of frontline practitioners who report adequate awareness of referral pathways, confidence in making referrals, and trust that referrals will be acted upon, used as a perceptual indicator of the human dimension of cross-sector coordination functionality.

Indicator statement: Proportion of frontline practitioners who report participating in at least one joint case discussion or review with a practitioner from a different sector for a shared learner or family in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of frontline practitioners who report participating in at least one joint case discussion or review involving a practitioner from a different sector for a shared learner or family in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a proxy for joint case management as the highest-order expression of cross-sector coordination.

Indicator statement: Whether at least one documented programme adaptation traceable to a collective learning or evidence review process occurred and was communicated to all active partners in the programme year
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether at least one documented programme adaptation traceable to a collective learning or evidence review process occurred and was communicated to all partners in the programme year, used as an outcome indicator for whether collective learning is translating into programme improvement.

Indicator statement: Equity gap between target share and actual participation rate for each pre-defined priority group as defined in the programme equity targeting framework, reported annually in percentage points
Indicator descriptor: Measures the difference in percentage points between the target share and the actual participation rate for each pre-defined priority group in the reporting year, used as an equity performance indicator for whether the programme's targeting strategy is achieving its defined equity commitments.

Indicator statement: Proportion of documented participation barrier mitigation plans due within the reference period for which verifiable documentary evidence of implementation exists on or before the target completion date
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of documented participation barrier mitigation plans whose target completion date falls within the reference period and for which verifiable documentary evidence of implementation exists, used as an implementation accountability indicator for the barrier reduction pipeline.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme participants who report experiencing exclusion or discriminatory treatment based on their identity in the programme context in the six months prior to the survey, disaggregated by identity dimension
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of programme participants who report experiencing exclusion, mockery, or differential treatment based on their identity in the programme context in the six months prior to the survey, used as a negative experience equity indicator and safeguarding signal disaggregated by identity dimension.
Sub-themes: Perceived Fairness and Sense of Belonging (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Fairness-and-Sense-of-Belonging-33e60bfb014e81c79ba6e8d41b2128e7?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Equity gap in percentage points between highest and lowest performing sub-groups across defined equity dimensions for each key programme outcome indicator, reported annually
Indicator descriptor: Measures the equity gap in percentage points between the highest and lowest performing sub-groups across defined equity dimensions for each key programme outcome indicator, used as the core analytical metric for disaggregated outcome equity monitoring.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme participants who report a strong sense of belonging, welcome, and being valued in the programme environment, measured on a structured instrument at the time of survey, disaggregated by priority group
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of programme participants who report a strong sense of belonging, welcome, and being valued in the programme environment, used as a foundational perceptual equity indicator of whether inclusion is being experienced in practice by participants from marginalised backgrounds.
Sub-themes: Perceived Fairness and Sense of Belonging (https://www.notion.so/Perceived-Fairness-and-Sense-of-Belonging-33e60bfb014e81c79ba6e8d41b2128e7?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of equity gaps exceeding the 15 percentage point threshold that have a documented programme response comprising an investigation, a specific adaptation, and communication to relevant staff and partners, in the reporting year
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of equity gaps exceeding the 15 percentage point threshold that have a documented programme response comprising an investigation, a specific adaptation, and communication to relevant staff and partners, used as an adaptive management accountability indicator for disaggregated outcome equity.

Indicator statement: Gender parity index for each programme activity type, computed as the ratio of female to male participants, reported annually by activity type and community
Indicator descriptor: Measures the gender parity index for each programme activity type, computed as the ratio of female to male participants, used as a structural equity indicator of whether girls and women are accessing programme activities at rates comparable to boys and men.

Indicator statement: Whether the programme disaggregates at least three key outcome indicators by at least three defined equity dimensions and reports equity gaps with narrative interpretation in its annual monitoring report
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether the programme systematically disaggregates at least three key outcome indicators by at least three defined equity dimensions and reports equity gaps with narrative interpretation in its annual monitoring report, used as an infrastructure indicator for disaggregated outcome equity monitoring.

Indicator statement: Gender gap in self-reported voice equity across speaking opportunity, contribution seriousness, and decision influence in programme group settings, measured on a structured instrument at the time of survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the gender gap in self-reported voice equity across speaking opportunity, contribution seriousness, and decision influence in programme group settings, used as a relational equity indicator of whether gender-equitable voice is being achieved in practice.

Indicator statement: Whether the programme's annual monitoring report includes at least one qualifying intersectional equity analysis combining two or more equity dimensions for a key outcome indicator
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether the programme's annual monitoring report includes at least one intersectional equity analysis combining two or more equity dimensions for a key outcome indicator and identifying whether compounding marginalisation produces a larger equity gap, used as an advanced equity monitoring indicator.

Indicator statement: Proportion of observed programme sessions in which the facilitator demonstrates at least four of five defined gender-responsive facilitation behaviours, assessed through structured observation across the programme year
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of observed programme sessions in which a facilitator demonstrates at least four of five defined gender-responsive facilitation behaviours, used as a practice quality indicator for the delivery mechanism through which gender equity commitments are operationalised at the point of participant contact.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme participants who report fair and equal treatment by facilitators and peers in the programme environment, measured on a structured instrument at the time of survey, disaggregated by priority group
Indicator descriptor: Measures the share of programme participants who report being treated fairly and with equal respect by programme facilitators and peers regardless of their identity, used as a relational equity indicator of perceived fairness of treatment in the programme environment.
