Indicator descriptor: Measures whether the school or programme site has a functioning formative assessment system meeting four defined criteria covering tool availability, recording systems, teacher use, and classroom observation confirmation, used as an assessment infrastructure indicator.

Indicator statement: Proportion of observed teaching sessions in which the teacher used at least one qualifying formative assessment practice with a visible instructional adjustment, assessed through structured classroom observation in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of observed teaching sessions in which the teacher used at least one qualifying formative assessment practice involving a deliberate understanding check followed by a visible instructional adjustment, used as a classroom assessment practice frequency indicator.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme teachers and facilitators who received at least one qualifying structured classroom observation and written feedback session from an instructional leader in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, years of experience, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme teachers and facilitators who received at least one qualifying structured classroom observation followed by written feedback from an instructional leader in the school term prior to the survey, used as an instructional leadership quality indicator.

Indicator statement: Whether the head teacher or programme manager made at least one documented data-informed instructional leadership decision grounded in learner assessment or monitoring data in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by site type and geographic area
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether the head teacher or programme manager made at least one documented data-informed instructional leadership decision grounded in learner assessment or monitoring data in the school term prior to the survey, used as an evidence-based leadership indicator.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme teachers and facilitators who report adequate professional self-efficacy across impact belief and responsive efficacy for struggling learners dimensions, assessed through anonymous annual survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme teachers and facilitators who report adequate professional self-efficacy across impact belief and responsive efficacy for struggling learners dimensions, used as a professional identity indicator for the motivational belief that sustains instructional effort.

Indicator statement: Proportion of observed teaching sessions in which at least 70 percent of learners demonstrate active engagement across defined observable behaviours, assessed through structured interval scan observation in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by teacher gender, teaching level, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of observed teaching sessions in which at least 70 percent of learners demonstrate active engagement across five defined observable behaviours, assessed through structured interval scan observation, used as a classroom interaction coverage indicator for broad learner participation.

Indicator statement: Proportion of sampled learner interactions in observed sessions classified as substantive cognitive engagement rather than surface compliance, assessed through structured classroom observation in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by teacher gender, teaching level, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of sampled learner interactions in observed sessions classified as substantive cognitive engagement rather than surface compliance, used as a classroom interaction depth indicator for the quality of learner participation.

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who report that participation opportunities are fairly distributed across all learners in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, age band, vulnerable group status, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of learners who report that participation opportunities are fairly distributed across all learners across equal chances and facilitator effort to include quieter learners dimensions, used as a classroom interaction equity indicator.

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who report adequate participation opportunity across frequency of chance to share and frequency of feeling invited and welcomed in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, age band, vulnerable group status, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of learners who report adequate participation opportunity across frequency of chance to share and frequency of feeling invited and welcomed in the school term prior to the survey, used as a learner experience indicator for the demand-side perception of classroom interaction quality.

Indicator statement: Proportion of observed teaching sessions that included at least two distinct types of qualifying interactive learning activity from at least two different taxonomy categories, assessed through structured classroom observation in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by teacher gender, years of experience, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of observed sessions that included at least two distinct types of qualifying interactive learning activity from at least two different taxonomy categories, used as a facilitation repertoire indicator for interactive activity type diversity.

Indicator statement: Proportion of observed interactive learning activities that met all three defined implementation fidelity criteria covering learner engagement breadth, interaction authenticity, and task completion, assessed through structured classroom observation in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by activity type and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of observed interactive learning activities that met all three implementation fidelity criteria covering learner engagement breadth, interaction authenticity, and task completion, used as a facilitation quality indicator for whether interactive activities produce genuine learner interaction.

Indicator statement: Proportion of individual learners observed making at least one qualifying verbal or task-based contribution in each observed session, assessed through individual learner tracking in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, vulnerable group status, seating position, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of individual learners who are observed making at least one qualifying verbal or task-based contribution in each observed session, used as a classroom interaction equity indicator for individual learner contribution equity as distinct from session-level participation breadth.

Indicator statement: Proportion of observed teaching sessions that included at least two qualifying interactive learner-engagement activities of any type from the programme taxonomy, assessed through structured classroom observation in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by teacher gender, years of experience, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of observed sessions that included at least two qualifying interactive learner-engagement activities of any type from the programme taxonomy, used as a facilitation baseline indicator for minimum structured learner engagement as a development stage marker preceding the more demanding category diversity standard in indicator 5a.

Indicator statement: Proportion of weekly lesson plans that include documented differentiated activities for at least two identified learner groups meeting four defined criteria, in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by teacher gender, years of experience, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of weekly lesson plans submitted in the school term that include documented differentiated activities for at least two identified learner groups meeting four defined quality criteria, used as a pedagogical planning indicator for the systematic advance planning of differentiated instruction.

Indicator statement: Proportion of observed teaching sessions in which the teacher implemented structured differentiated provision for at least two distinct learner groups meeting three defined criteria, assessed through structured classroom observation in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by teacher gender, years of experience, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of observed sessions in which the teacher implemented structured differentiated provision for at least two distinct learner groups meeting three defined criteria, used as a pedagogical practice indicator for the classroom-level delivery of differentiated instruction.

Indicator statement: Proportion of documented structured formative assessment records that have at least one qualifying documented instructional follow-up action directly addressing identified learner findings in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, years of experience, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of documented structured formative assessment records in the school term that have at least one documented instructional follow-up action directly addressing identified learner findings and specifying an implementation timeframe, used as a teaching practice quality indicator for assessment-to-instruction translation.
Sub-themes: Use of assessments to support  learning (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-assessments-to-support-learning-31260bfb014e809da07be351d7091f33?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme teachers and facilitators who produced at least two qualifying documented structured formative assessment records with individual or group learner findings and learning implications in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, years of experience, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme teachers and facilitators who produced at least two qualifying documented structured formative assessment records including individual or group learner findings and learning implications in the school term prior to the survey, used as a teaching practice indicator for formative assessment documentation.
Sub-themes: Use of assessments to support  learning (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-assessments-to-support-learning-31260bfb014e809da07be351d7091f33?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme teachers and facilitators who conducted and documented at least the programme-defined minimum number of qualifying formative assessments in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, years of experience, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme teachers and facilitators who conducted and documented at least the programme-defined minimum number of qualifying formative assessments in the school term prior to the survey, used as a classroom assessment practice indicator for formative assessment frequency and documentation.
Sub-themes: Use of assessments to support  learning (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-assessments-to-support-learning-31260bfb014e809da07be351d7091f33?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of documented formative assessments in the school term that produced a qualifying documented instructional follow-up action within five teaching days directly addressing a specific assessment finding, disaggregated by action type, teacher gender, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of documented formative assessments in the school term that produced a qualifying documented instructional follow-up action within five teaching days directly addressing a specific assessment finding, used as a teaching responsiveness indicator for assessment-to-action follow-through.
Sub-themes: Use of assessments to support  learning (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-assessments-to-support-learning-31260bfb014e809da07be351d7091f33?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of enrolled learners who received at least one qualifying piece of individual formative feedback linked to a specific assessment finding in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, age band, vulnerable group status, and learning level
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of enrolled learners who received at least one qualifying piece of individual formative feedback linked to a specific assessment finding in the school term prior to the survey, used as a feedback equity indicator for individual progress feedback receipt.
Sub-themes: Use of assessments to support  learning (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-assessments-to-support-learning-31260bfb014e809da07be351d7091f33?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of learners who received individual formative feedback and report clearly understanding both their strengths and their improvement areas from that feedback, in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, age band, and learning level
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of learners who received individual formative feedback and report clearly understanding both what they were doing well and what they needed to do differently, used as a feedback effectiveness indicator for learner feedback comprehension.
Sub-themes: Use of assessments to support  learning (https://www.notion.so/Use-of-assessments-to-support-learning-31260bfb014e809da07be351d7091f33?pvs=21)

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who demonstrate proficiency at or above the programme-defined standard in at least two of three assessed transferable skill domains at end of school year, derived from domain-level assessments, disaggregated by gender, age band, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who demonstrate proficiency at or above the programme-defined standard in at least two of three assessed skill domains, communication, problem-solving, and digital skills, at end of school year, used as a composite summary indicator derived from the three domain-specific proficiency indicators.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who demonstrate proficiency at or above the locally calibrated programme-defined standard across at least two of three defined digital skill areas at end of school year, disaggregated by gender, age band, vulnerable group status, and device access level
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who demonstrate digital skills proficiency at or above a locally calibrated programme-defined standard across at least two of three defined skill areas at end of school year, used as a domain-specific transferable skill indicator for digital skills.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who demonstrate proficiency at or above the programme-defined standard on both oral and written communication components at end of school year, disaggregated by gender, age band, vulnerable group status, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who demonstrate communication proficiency at or above the programme-defined standard on both oral and written components at end of school year, used as a domain-specific transferable skill indicator for communication.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who demonstrate proficiency at or above the programme-defined standard on a structured unfamiliar problem-solving task across four defined criteria at end of school year, disaggregated by gender, age band, vulnerable group status, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who demonstrate problem-solving proficiency at or above the programme-defined standard across four criteria on an unfamiliar structured task at end of school year, used as a domain-specific transferable skill indicator for problem-solving.

Indicator statement: Proportion of at-standard integrated task completions in which both digital information use and collaborative problem-solving were genuinely integrated across two defined integration criteria, assessed through structured assessor observation at end of school year
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of at-standard integrated task completions in which both digital information use and collaborative problem-solving were genuinely integrated rather than sequentially divided between individual learners, used as a skill integration quality indicator for applied combined skill use.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who complete a structured integrated task requiring digital information use and collaborative problem-solving at or above the programme-defined performance standard on all three rubric dimensions at end of school year, disaggregated by gender, age band, vulnerable group status, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who complete a structured integrated task requiring digital information use and collaborative problem-solving at or above the programme-defined performance standard on all three rubric dimensions, used as an applied skills indicator for integrated task performance.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth classified as medium or high contributors by observer and who self-rate as having contributed their fair share during a structured group task, disaggregated by gender, age band, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who demonstrate adequate shared responsibility in group work, assessed through observer contribution distribution classification and self-assessment of fair contribution, used as a collaborative culture indicator.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who report participating in at least one qualifying civic structure or process in the 12 months prior to the survey, verified through naming, disaggregated by gender, age band, and community
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who report participating in at least one qualifying civic structure or process in the 12 months prior to the survey, used as a civic skills indicator for civic participation behaviour beyond the programme.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who provide a qualifying structured reflection across all three prompts of a structured reflection protocol administered after a learning activity, disaggregated by gender, age band, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who provide a qualifying structured reflection response across three prompts covering specific learning, facilitating conditions, and future change, used as a metacognitive indicator for learning reflection quality.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who demonstrate learning persistence under difficulty across a qualifying scenario response and school term frequency self-report, in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, age band, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who demonstrate learning persistence under difficulty across a scenario-based choice item and a frequency self-report item, used as a self-regulation indicator for persistence as a learning disposition.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who report applying at least one specific programme-developed skill outside the programme in the 12 months prior to the survey, verified through naming, situation description, and action description, disaggregated by gender, age band, and skill type
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who report applying at least one specific programme-developed skill in an outside-programme context in the 12 months prior to the survey, verified through a three-element description requirement, used as a skill transfer self-report indicator.

Indicator statement: Proportion of observed programme youth who made at least two distinct types of qualifying collaborative contribution during a structured group task, assessed through observer tracking in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, age band, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of observed programme youth who made at least two distinct types of qualifying collaborative contribution during a structured group task, used as a collaborative skills indicator for breadth and quality of group task contribution.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who correctly identify a specific applicable programme skill with a plausible explanation for at least two of three out-of-context scenarios, assessed through structured recognition task at end of school year, disaggregated by gender, age band, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who correctly identify a specific applicable programme skill and provide a plausible explanation for at least two of three out-of-context scenarios, used as an applied skills indicator for the awareness precondition of skill transfer.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who report regular self-monitoring and consistent strategy adjustment when their learning approach is not working, in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, age band, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who report regularly noticing when their learning strategy is not working and consistently trying a different approach, used as a metacognitive awareness and adaptive regulation indicator.

Indicator statement: Proportion of observed programme youth who demonstrate at least one qualifying constructive disagreement response during a structured group discussion or debate activity, assessed through observer tracking in the school term prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of observed programme youth who demonstrate at least one qualifying constructive disagreement response during a structured group discussion or debate activity, used as a collaborative skills indicator for constructive disagreement and perspective-taking.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme completers for whom a nominated community contact describes a specific observed skill application and confirms it was useful or made a positive difference, in the 12 months prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, age band, and community
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme completers for whom a nominated community contact describes a specific observed behaviour corresponding to a programme-developed skill and confirms it was useful or made a positive difference, used as an external validation indicator for skill transfer recognition.

Indicator statement: Proportion of programme youth who set a qualifying specific learning goal with a named action and success indicator or timeframe, assessed through structured individual task in the school term prior to the survey, disaggregated by gender, age band, and site
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of programme youth who can set at least one specific, realistic, and time-bound learning goal with a named action and success indicator, used as a self-regulation indicator for the foundational planning competency of directed learning.

Indicator statement: Operational cost coverage ratio — proportion of Learning Centre running costs covered by commercial income in the fiscal year
Indicator descriptor: The ratio of total commercial income (space rental, tenancy, and bespoke training) to total Learning Centre running costs in the fiscal year, expressed as a percentage, reported quarterly with year-on-year trend.

Indicator statement: Number of community learning sessions delivered in the reporting year, reported separately for sessions delivered directly by TFFT and by tenant partners
Indicator descriptor: Counts the number of qualifying community learning sessions delivered in the reporting year, reported separately for sessions delivered directly by TFFT and sessions delivered by tenant partner organisations using the Learning Centre space.

Indicator statement: Annual income from bespoke organisational training in the fiscal year
Indicator descriptor: Total confirmed received income attributable to bespoke organisational training commissions in the fiscal year, reported with year-on-year trend.

Indicator statement: Annual income from space rental and tenancy agreements in the fiscal year
Indicator descriptor: Total confirmed received income attributable to space rental and tenancy agreements in the fiscal year, reported with year-on-year trend.

Indicator statement: Number of individual learners accessing free programmes through the Learning Centre in the reporting year, disaggregated by age, gender, and vulnerability status
Indicator descriptor: Counts the number of unique individuals who accessed at least one free learning programme through the Learning Centre in the reporting year, disaggregated by age band, gender, and vulnerability status, reported separately for TFFT-delivered and tenant-delivered reach.

Indicator statement: Total reach of the Learning Centre in the reporting year, including learners served by tenant organisations using the space
Indicator descriptor: Aggregates the total number of learners reached through the Learning Centre in the reporting year, combining direct TFFT programme reach and reach generated by tenant organisations using the space.

Indicator statement: Family ripple effect of scholar support — proportion of scholar households in which a caregiver reports a positive change in other household members' educational attitudes, behaviours, or learning participation attributable to the scholar's TFFT experience, in the 12 months prior to the survey
Indicator descriptor: Measures the extent to which a scholar's participation in TFFT programmes positively influences the educational attitudes, behaviours, and aspirations of their immediate family members, assessed through annual caregiver household survey.

Indicator statement: Number of formal policy submissions or community advocacy campaigns led by project youth at national carbon registry hearings or international climate summits.
Proportion of project-enrolled youth with individual mobile money accounts or micro-finance access linked to direct carbon dividend payments.
Proportion of youth participants demonstrating a 'High' competency rating in NbS application and climate-risk assessment through standardized post-training assessment.
Number of youth-led 'spin-off' businesses funded or supported by carbon revenue (e.g., seedling nurseries, sustainable honey, or eco-tourism).
Proportion of youth participants attaining certifications in nature-based solutions through project activities.
Number of youth representatives formally involved in the end-to-end Project Cycle (specifically in planning, monitoring, and reporting phases).
Ratio of youth (under 30) with voting rights on Project Steering Committees or Benefit-Sharing Boards.
Rate of adoption of digital conservation tools (e.g., dMRV apps, drone-based mapping, and remote sensing platforms) among project youth.

Number and type of reported human-wildlife conflict incidents (crop raiding, livestock predation, property damage, or direct attack) recorded within the project boundary per reporting period, disaggregated by incident type and location.
Indicator descriptor: Tracks the frequency and nature of human-wildlife conflict events over time to assess whether project mitigation interventions are reducing conflict incidence.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members within the project boundary who report feeling physically safe and whose daily activities are not significantly restricted by fear of wildlife encounters, at the time of survey.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the physical safety and psychological wellbeing dimensions of human-wildlife conflict, capturing both objective risk exposure and subjective fear burden on community members.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households that submitted a compensation or insurance claim for human-wildlife conflict losses and received a fair and timely payment within the scheme's defined resolution period.
Indicator descriptor: Evaluates the delivery performance of compensation and insurance schemes by measuring whether affected households receive adequate, fair, and timely redress for losses.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members within the project boundary who report that project conservation or land-use activities have increased wildlife movement into farming or residential areas, intensifying human-wildlife conflict.
Indicator descriptor: Monitors whether project-driven land-use changes and habitat protection activities are displacing wildlife into community-use areas and inadvertently increasing conflict at the human-wildlife interface.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households within the project boundary reporting significant crop, livestock, or property losses attributable to human-wildlife conflict in the 12 months prior to the survey.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the breadth and severity of livelihood impacts caused by human-wildlife conflict, tracking whether project activities reduce the economic burden on affected households.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project-area communities with a documented and functioning human safety response system — including at minimum an early warning mechanism and a designated response protocol for dangerous wildlife encounters — verified during the reporting period.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether communities have functioning infrastructure and response capacity to protect people from dangerous wildlife encounters, moving beyond perception of safety to assess the presence of tangible protective measures.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members within the project boundary who report that fear or stress related to human-wildlife conflict significantly affects their psychological wellbeing or daily functioning, at the time of survey.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the psychological burden associated with living alongside wildlife conflict, capturing the stress and anxiety dimension that goes beyond physical safety to affect daily wellbeing and mental health.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project-area communities with at least one functioning human-wildlife conflict mitigation measure — such as deterrents, early warning systems, or community patrol arrangements — verified as operational during the reporting period.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the coverage and functionality of physical and community-based HWC deterrent and mitigation infrastructure, tracking whether the project is building effective conflict prevention capacity at the community level.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households within the project boundary who are aware of the human-wildlife conflict compensation or insurance scheme and report being able to access it without significant barriers, at the time of survey.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether community members are aware of and able to access compensation or insurance schemes without significant barriers, as a precondition for scheme uptake and equitable benefit delivery.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project-area communities with an active, community-led system for monitoring and reporting changes in wildlife movement patterns to project management or relevant authorities, verified as operational during the reporting period.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether communities are actively engaged in monitoring wildlife movement and reporting changes that may signal increased conflict risk, tracking the presence of community-led early warning systems at the wildlife-community interface.

Indicator statement: Proportion of households that experienced significant human-wildlife conflict losses and report having substantially recovered their income or food security within 6 months of the loss event.
Indicator descriptor: Measures household resilience and recovery capacity following HWC-related losses, tracking whether project-supported livelihood diversification and compensation mechanisms enable households to recover within a reasonable timeframe.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members within the project boundary who report having been meaningfully consulted about land-use changes — such as forest protection zones or wildlife corridor designations — that affect where wildlife moves and where human-wildlife conflict may occur.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the quality of community participation in land-use planning decisions that affect the wildlife-community interface, tracking whether affected communities have genuine input into decisions that may increase wildlife displacement.

Indicator statement: Average estimated monetary value of crop, livestock, and property losses per household attributable to human-wildlife conflict in the 12 months prior to the survey, disaggregated by loss type and household vulnerability status.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the economic magnitude of HWC losses at the household level, providing a severity metric that goes beyond incidence counts to capture actual financial impact on livelihoods.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members who are aware of and report being able to use an accessible appeals or dispute resolution process for human-wildlife conflict compensation decisions they believe to be unfair or incorrect.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether households have meaningful recourse when compensation claims are denied or disputed, testing the accountability and fairness dimension of scheme governance.

Indicator statement: Proportion of community members within the project boundary who report a reduction in the frequency of human-wildlife conflict incidents since the project began.
Indicator descriptor: Measures community perception of whether human-wildlife conflict incidents have decreased as a result of project mitigation activities, providing a community-level validation of administrative incident records.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project employment roles filled by local community members, disaggregated by gender, Indigenous identity, disability status, and other marginalised group categories, through a documented, transparent, and non-discriminatory hiring process.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether project recruitment practices are equitable and locally anchored, tracking both the procedural fairness of hiring processes and the proportion of roles filled by community members, women, Indigenous Peoples, and other marginalised groups.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who are aware of a functioning worker grievance mechanism, report confidence in using it without fear of retaliation, and describe worker-management relations as transparent and fair.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether workers are aware of, can access, and trust the grievance mechanism, and whether the worker-management relationship is characterised by transparency, fairness, and accountability.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who report that their compensation is fair, equitable across gender and identity groups, and accompanied by employment benefits consistent with decent work standards.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether project workers receive equitable, transparent, and fair wages and employment conditions that meet recognised decent work standards, disaggregated by gender and identity group to detect pay gaps.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who confirm receiving occupational health and safety training before commencing work and report consistent access to personal protective equipment and a safe working environment.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether project workers have received adequate health and safety training, have access to protective equipment, and operate in a safe workplace, as well as whether the project invests in worker professional development.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who report having access to relevant skills development, training, or professional capacity-building opportunities through their project employment.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether the project invests in worker skills development and professional capacity building — a distinct construct from occupational safety that tracks economic and career development benefits of employment.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who raised a work-related concern or complaint and report that it was resolved fairly and promptly, with no retaliation experienced.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the quality of grievance resolution outcomes — specifically whether complaints are resolved fairly and promptly without retaliation — as distinct from awareness of or access to the mechanism.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who are aware of a worker grievance mechanism and report confidence in using it safely without fear of negative consequences.
Indicator descriptor: Measures worker awareness of and stated confidence in using the grievance mechanism without fear of retaliation — access and psychological safety are prerequisites for the mechanism to function, and must be measured before resolution quality.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project employment roles filled by local community members within the project boundary, disaggregated by gender, Indigenous identity, and disability status.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the proportion of project employment roles filled by people from the local community, disaggregated by gender, Indigenous identity, and disability status. Tracks whether the project prioritises local hiring as an economic benefit to host communities.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who report that compensation for equivalent roles is equitable and transparent across gender, ethnicity, age, and other identity groups, with no unexplained pay gaps.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether wages are equitable across gender and other identity groups for workers in equivalent roles. Directly tests for discriminatory pay gaps rather than overall wage satisfaction.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who describe the relationship between workers and management as transparent, respectful, and governed by fair and accountable processes.
Indicator descriptor: Measures the overall quality of worker-management relations as a relational outcome distinct from the grievance mechanism — whether the relationship is characterised by transparency, respect, and accountability.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who report that their workplace is consistently safe, with hazards proactively identified and addressed and health and safety standards actively enforced.
Indicator descriptor: Measures workers' ongoing experience of physical workplace safety — whether hazards are proactively managed and standards enforced — as a distinct outcome from pre-work training compliance.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project employment roles filled through a documented, transparent, and non-discriminatory hiring process within the reporting period.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether the project's recruitment process is transparent, documented, and free from discrimination. Tracks procedural quality rather than the outcome of who was hired.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who report that their employment conditions meet decent work standards, including access to employment benefits such as leave and health support, job security, and dignified treatment free from discrimination.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether project employment conditions meet recognised decent work standards beyond pay — including job security, employment benefits, and dignified treatment — as distinct from pay equity.

Indicator statement: Proportion of project workers who confirm receiving occupational health and safety training and access to required personal protective equipment before commencing project work.
Indicator descriptor: Measures whether workers received safety training and were issued personal protective equipment before starting work — a pre-deployment process compliance indicator distinct from ongoing workplace safety conditions.
